name: Galarian Perrserker

attacks.name: Call to Muster
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: After many battles, it evolved dangerous claws that come together to form daggers when extended.

name: Excadrill

attacks.name: Rock Tomb
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Known as the Drill King, this Pokémon can tunnel through the terrain at speeds of over 90 mph.

name: Genesect V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Fusion Strike System
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have as many cards in your hand as you have Fusion Strike Pokémon in play.

attacks.name: Techno Blast
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Togedemaru

attacks.name: Let's All Rollout
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Let's All Rollout attack.

flavorText: When it's in trouble, it curls up into a ball, makes its fur spikes stand on end, and then discharges electricity indiscriminately.

name: Klefki

attacks.name: Unlock
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: Long ago it lived in mines, but once the minerals that make up its diet became scarcer, Klefki began appearing in human settlements.

name: Melmetal

attacks.name: Ingot Swing
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon that have an Ability.

attacks.name: Blasting Hammer
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Revered long ago for its capacity to create iron from nothing, for some reason it has come back to life after 3,000 years.

name: Meltan

attacks.name: Iron Intake
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It melts particles of iron and other metals found in the subsoil, so it can absorb them into its body of molten steel.

name: Corviknight

attacks.name: Steel Wing
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Power Cyclone
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: With their great intellect and flying skills, these Pokémon very successfully act as the Galar region's airborne taxi service.

name: Cufant

attacks.name: High Horsepower
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: If a job requires serious strength, this Pokémon will excel at it. Its copper body tarnishes in the rain, turning a vibrant green color.

name: Copperajah

attacks.name: High Horsepower
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: These Pokémon live in herds. Their trunks have incredible grip strength, strong enough to crush giant rocks into powder.

name: Latias

abilities.name: Red Assist
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Psychic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Latios.

attacks.name: Dyna Barrier
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon VMAX.

flavorText: It can telepathically communicate with people. It changes its appearance using its down that refracts light.

name: Latios

abilities.name: Blue Assist
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Psychic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Latias.

attacks.name: Luster Purge
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It understands human speech and is highly intelligent. It is a tender Pokémon that dislikes fighting.

name: Goomy
flavorText: Their horns are powerful sensors. As soon as Goomy pick up any sign of enemies, they go into hiding. This is how they've survived.

name: Goodra

abilities.name: Slimy Room
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent tries to attach an Energy card from their hand to a Pokémon, they must flip a coin. If tails, your opponent discards that Energy card instead of attaching it, and this doesn't use up their Energy attachment for the turn.

flavorText: Its form of offense is forcefully stretching out its horns. The strikes land 100 times harder than any blow from a heavyweight boxer.

name: Sliggoo

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The lump on its back contains its tiny brain. It thinks only of food and escaping its enemies.

name: Persian

attacks.name: Pay Day
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: Its elegant and refined behavior clashes with that of the barbaric Perrserker. The relationship between the two is one of mutual disdain.

name: Turtonator

attacks.name: Shell Trap
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

flavorText: Eating sulfur in its volcanic habitat is what causes explosive compounds to develop in its shell. Its droppings are also dangerously explosive.

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Pay Day
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It washes its face regularly to keep the coin on its forehead spotless. It doesn't get along with Galarian Meowth.

name: Dodrio V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: No Reprieve
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's attacks do 80 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Rampage Drill
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Chansey

attacks.name: Drain Slap
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Because the eggs on their bellies have been overharvested by people in the past, the Chansey population remains very small.

name: Blissey

abilities.name: Expert in Roundness
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to each of your Pokémon that has the Let's All Rollout attack by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon VMAX.

attacks.name: Let's All Rollout
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Let's All Rollout attack.

flavorText: This Pokémon is overflowing with love. Only Chansey that share a strong bond with their Trainer can evolve, so people say.

name: Kangaskhan

attacks.name: Coordinated One-Two Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: You shouldn't get close to the child when it's playing outside its mother's pouch. Its mother is always nearby watching over it.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Continuous Steps
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

name: Dunsparce

abilities.name: Mysterious Nest
abilities.text: Colorless Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Weakness.

flavorText: When it sees a person, it digs a hole with its tail to make its escape. If you happen to find one, consider yourself lucky.

name: Stantler

attacks.name: Wild Dive
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The curved antlers subtly change the flow of air to create a strange space where reality is distorted.

name: Skitty

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It shows its cute side by chasing its own tail until it gets dizzy.

name: Smeargle

attacks.name: Sketching Trash
attacks.text: Put up to 2 Fusion Strike Trainer cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: It draws symbols with the fluid that oozes from the tip of its tail. Depending on the symbol, Smeargle fanatics will pay big money for them.

name: Snorlax
flavorText: This Pokémon's stomach is so strong, even eating moldy or rotten food will not affect it.

name: Delcatty

attacks.name: Willful Busybody
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a card you find there and put it on the bottom of their deck.

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It is highly popular among female Trainers for its sublime fur. It does not keep a nest.

name: Buneary

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The reason it keeps one ear rolled up is so it can launch a swift counterattack if it's attacked by an enemy.

name: Lopunny
flavorText: It's notably wary and has a dislike of fighting, but at the same time, it can deliver powerful kicks with its lithe legs.

name: Diggersby

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: The fur on its belly retains heat exceptionally well. People used to make heavy winter clothing from fur shed by this Pokémon.

name: Hawlucha

attacks.name: Flying Stomp
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It always strikes a pose before going for its finishing move. Sometimes opponents take advantage of that time to counterattack.

name: Bunnelby

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It's very sensitive to danger. The sound of Corviknight's flapping will have Bunnelby digging a hole to hide underground in moments.

name: Corvisquire

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The lessons of many harsh battles have taught it how to accurately judge an opponent's strength.

name: Greedent VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Turn a Profit
attacks.text: If your opponent's Basic Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 2 more Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Gimme Gimme
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

name: Greedent V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Nom-Nom-Nom Incisors
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

name: Rookidee

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Jumping nimbly about, this small-bodied Pokémon takes advantage of even the slightest opportunity to disorient larger opponents.

name: Wooloo

attacks.name: Derail
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Let's All Rollout
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Let's All Rollout attack.

flavorText: If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won't be able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this Pokémon is surprisingly strong.

name: Dubwool

attacks.name: Bounce
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its majestic horns are meant only to impress the opposite gender. They never see use in battle.

name: Wooloo

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won't be able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this Pokémon is surprisingly strong.

name: Adventurer's Discovery
rules: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon V, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Battle VIP Pass
rules: You can use this card only during your first turn.
Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Bug Catcher
rules: Draw 2 cards. Flip a coin. If heads, draw 2 more cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Cram-o-matic
rules: You can use this card only if you discard another Item card from your hand. Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Cook
rules: Heal 70 damage from your Active Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Cross Switcher
rules: You must play 2 Cross Switcher cards at once. (This effect works one time for 2 cards.)
Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. If you do, switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Chili & Cilan & Cress
rules: Search your deck for up to 3 Fusion Strike Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Crossceiver
rules: You must play 2 Crossceiver cards at once. (This effect works one time for 2 cards.)
Put a Pokémon or a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Dancer
rules: Draw 2 cards. If you go second and it's your first turn, draw 3 more cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Farewell Bell
rules: If the Pokémon VMAX this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Judge
rules: Each player shuffles their hand into their deck and draws 4 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Elesa's Sparkle
rules: Choose up to 2 of your Fusion Strike Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a Fusion Strike Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Power Tablet
rules: During this turn, your Fusion Strike Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Quick Ball
rules: You can play this card only if you discard another card from your hand. Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Schoolboy
rules: Draw 2 cards. If your opponent has exactly 1, 3, or 5 Prize cards remaining, draw 2 more cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Shauna
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Schoolgirl
rules: Draw 2 cards. If your opponent has exactly 2, 4, or 6 Prize cards remaining, draw 2 more cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Skaters' Park
rules: Whenever either player's Active Pokémon retreats, put any basic Energy that would be discarded into their hand instead of the discard pile.

name: Sidney
rules: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard up to 2 in any combination of Pokémon Tool cards, Special Energy cards, and Stadium cards from it.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Spongy Gloves
rules: The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Water Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Fusion Strike Energy
rules: This card can only be attached to a Fusion Strike Pokémon. If this card is attached to anything other than a Fusion Strike Pokémon, discard this card. As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. Prevent all effects of your opponent's Pokémon's Abilities done to the Pokémon this card is attached to.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Queen's Orders
attacks.text: You may discard any number of your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 40 more damage for each Benched Pokémon you discarded in this way.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psychic Leap
attacks.text: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Poltergeist
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 40 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

name: Crabominable V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Trigger Avalanche
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Destroyer Punch
attacks.text: This attack does 60 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Boltund V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Smash Turn
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Electrobullet
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Hoopa V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shadow Impact
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on 1 of your Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

name: Flaaffy

abilities.name: Dynamotor
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It stores electricity in its fluffy fleece. If it stores up too much, it will start to go bald in those patches.

name: Pikachu on the Ball

attacks.name: Lightning Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.

name: Eevee on the Ball

attacks.name: Make the Assist
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.

name: Grookey on the Ball

attacks.name: Tenacious Defense
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.

name: Scorbunny on the Ball

attacks.name: Fiery Dribbling
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: A warm-up of running around gets fire energy coursing through this Pokémon's body. Once that happens, it's ready to fight at full power.

name: Sobble on the Ball

attacks.name: Spectacular Save
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: When scared, this Pokémon cries. Its tears pack the chemical punch of 100 onions, and attackers won't be able to resist weeping.

name: Pikachu VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Volt Tackle
attacks.text: You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 150 more damage.

name: Dragapult
rules: (This card cannot be used at official tournaments.)

attacks.name: Mach Turn
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Diving Swipe
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Dark Sylveon V

attacks.name: Disarming Voice
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Tricky Ribbon
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

name: Zacian LV.X

attacks.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Hex Maniac
rules: Until the end of your opponent's next turn, each Pokémon in play, in each player's hand, and in each player's discard pile has no Abilities. (This includes cards that come into play on that turn.)
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Leafeon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Leaf Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Slashing Strike
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Slashing Strike.

name: Leafeon VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Ivy Star
abilities.text: During your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Leaf Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Glaceon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Frost Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Water Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

name: Glaceon VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Icicle Shot
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Crystal Star
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Exeggcute
flavorText: Though it may look like it's just a bunch of eggs, it's a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

name: Exeggutor

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Each of Exeggutor's three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don't seem to be very interested in one another.

name: Shroomish
flavorText: It spouts poison spores from the top of its head. These spores cause pain all over if inhaled.

name: Breloom

attacks.name: Spore Ball
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Powdery Uppercut
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if this Pokémon used Spore Ball during your last turn.

flavorText: It scatters poisonous spores and throws powerful punches while its foe is hampered by inhaled spores.

name: Tropius

abilities.name: Curative Bower
abilities.text: All of your Pokémon that have Grass Energy attached can't be Confused, and if they are already Confused, they recover from that Special Condition.

flavorText: Bunches of delicious fruit grow around its neck. In warm areas, many ranches raise Tropius.

name: Turtwig
flavorText: It undertakes photosynthesis with its body, making oxygen. The leaf on its head wilts if it is thirsty.

name: Grotle

abilities.name: Sun-Drenched Shell
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Grass Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It knows where pure water wells up. It carries fellow Pokémon there on its back.

name: Torterra

attacks.name: Evopress
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each of your Evolution Pokémon in play.

flavorText: Small Pokémon occasionally gather on its unmoving back to begin building their nests.

name: Burmy
flavorText: If its cloak is broken in battle, it quickly remakes the cloak with materials nearby.

name: Wormadam

attacks.name: Matron's Anger
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile.

attacks.name: Leaf Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When Burmy evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon's body. The cloak is never shed.

name: Mothim

attacks.name: Raid
attacks.text: If this Pokémon evolved from Burmy during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: It flutters around at night and steals honey from the Combee hive.

name: Cherubi

abilities.name: Lively Fruit
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks from your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)

flavorText: The deeper a Cherubi's red, the more nutrients it has stockpiled in its body. And the sweeter and tastier its small ball!

name: Shaymin V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Revenge Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

name: Shaymin VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Star Bloom
abilities.text: During your turn, you may heal 120 damage from each of your Benched Grass Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Revenge Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

name: Karrablast
flavorText: Its strange physiology reacts to electrical energy in interesting ways. The presence of a Shelmet will cause this Pokémon to evolve.

name: Zarude V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Leap to Leap
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Jungle Rage
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 120 more damage.

name: Charizard V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Incinerate
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Charizard VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Explosive Fire
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.

attacks.name: Star Blaze
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Magmar
flavorText: Magmar dispatches its prey with fire. But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.

name: Magmortar

attacks.name: Boltsplosion
attacks.text: If Electivire is on your Bench, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: When Magmortar inhales deeply, the fire burning in its belly intensifies, rising in temperature to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Moltres

attacks.name: Inferno Wings
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 70 more damage. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

flavorText: It's one of the legendary bird Pokémon. When Moltres flaps its flaming wings, they glimmer with a dazzling red glow.

name: Entei V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Fleet-Footed
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may draw a card.

attacks.name: Burning Rondo
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

name: Torkoal

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It burns coal inside its shell for energy. It blows out black soot if it is endangered.

name: Chimchar

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The gas made in its belly burns from its rear end. The fire burns weakly when it feels sick.

name: Monferno

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses ceilings and walls to launch aerial attacks. Its fiery tail is but one weapon.

name: Infernape

attacks.name: Infernal Vortex
attacks.text: Reveal the top 5 cards of your deck. This attack does 80 damage for each Energy card you find there. Then, discard those Energy cards and shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

attacks.name: Burning Kick
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It tosses its enemies around with agility. It uses all its limbs to fight in its own unique style.

name: Simisear V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bursting Power
attacks.text: You may attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Flare Juggling
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Kingler V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Falling Bubbles
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 5 Water Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Raging Pincer
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Kingler VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bubbles Galore
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 5 Water Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: G-Max Pincer
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Staryu

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: If you visit a beach at the end of summer, you'll be able to see groups of Staryu lighting up in a steady rhythm.

name: Lapras

attacks.name: Raging Freeze
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon during their last turn, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

name: Corphish
flavorText: No matter how dirty the water in the river, it will adapt and thrive. It has a strong will to survive.

name: Crawdaunt

attacks.name: Crab Impact
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: A rough customer that wildly flails its giant claws. It is said to be extremely hard to raise.

name: Snorunt

attacks.name: Ice Breath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It can only survive in cold areas. It bounces happily around, even in environments as cold as -150 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Piplup

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Because it is very proud, it hates accepting food from people. Its thick down guards it from cold.

name: Prinplup
flavorText: It lives a solitary life. Its wings deliver wicked blows that can snap even the thickest of trees.

name: Empoleon

abilities.name: Emergency Surfacing
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in your discard pile and you have no cards in your hand, you may put this Pokémon onto your Bench. If you do, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The three horns that extend from its beak attest to its power. The leader has the biggest horns.

name: Buizel

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It inflates the flotation sac around its neck and pokes its head out of the water to see what is going on.

name: Floatzel

attacks.name: Floatify
attacks.text: Put up to 2 Item cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Its flotation sac developed as a result of pursuing aquatic prey. It can double as a rubber raft.

name: Lumineon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Luminous Sign
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Aqua Return
attacks.text: Shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

name: Manaphy

abilities.name: Wave Veil
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: It starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.

name: Cubchoo
flavorText: When this Pokémon is in good health, its snot becomes thicker and stickier. It will smear its snot on anyone it doesn't like.

name: Beartic

attacks.name: Sheer Cold
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It swims through frigid seas, searching for prey. From its frozen breath, it forms icy fangs that are harder than steel.

name: Eiscue

attacks.name: Block Slider
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Fusion Strike Energy attached to all of your Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The hair on its head connects to the surface of its brain. When this Pokémon has something on its mind, its hair chills the air around it.

name: Raichu V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fast Charge
attacks.text: If you go first, you can use this attack during your first turn. Search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Dynamic Spark
attacks.text: You may discard any amount of Lightning Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Electabuzz

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.

name: Electivire

attacks.name: Explosive Bolt
attacks.text: If any of your Benched Magmortar have any damage counters on them, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: High-Voltage Current
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The amount of electrical energy this Pokémon produces is proportional to the rate of its pulse. The voltage jumps while Electivire is battling.

name: Raikou V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Fleet-Footed
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may draw a card.

attacks.name: Lightning Rondo
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

name: Shinx
flavorText: This Pokémon generates electricity by contracting its muscles. Excited trembling is a sign that Shinx is generating a tremendous amount of electricity.

name: Luxio

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: By joining its tail with that of another Luxio, this Pokémon can receive some of the other Luxio's electricity and power up its own electric blasts.

name: Luxray

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flash Impact
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Luxray can see through solid objects. It will instantly spot prey trying to hide behind walls, even if the walls are thick.

name: Pachirisu

attacks.name: Windup Thunder
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

name: Clefairy
flavorText: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

name: Clefable

attacks.name: Moonlit Miracle
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. Choose a number of your Pokémon in play up to the number of heads. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

name: Starmie

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon has an organ known as its core. The organ glows in seven colors when Starmie is unleashing its potent psychic powers.

name: Mewtwo

attacks.name: Life Sucker
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew's genes. It's said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

name: Granbull V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Chomp
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bull Dash
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Baltoy

attacks.name: Triple Spin
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It moves while spinning around on its single foot. Some Baltoy have been seen spinning on their heads.

name: Claydol

attacks.name: Coinciding Figures
attacks.text: If you and your opponent have the same number of Benched Pokémon, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: This mysterious Pokémon started life as an ancient clay figurine made over 20,000 years ago.

name: Duskull

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: If it finds bad children who won't listen to their parents, it will spirit them away—or so it's said.

name: Dusclops

attacks.name: Fade to Black
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its body is entirely hollow. When it opens its mouth, it sucks everything in as if it were a black hole.

name: Dusknoir

abilities.name: Special Transfer
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a Special Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

flavorText: At the bidding of transmissions from the spirit world, it steals people and Pokémon away. No one knows whether it has a will of its own.

name: Chimecho

attacks.name: Clear Tone
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Special Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Emitting ultrasonic cries, it floats on winds to travel great distances.

name: Whimsicott V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fluff Gets in the Way
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Cotton Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Whimsicott VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Trick Wind
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Pokémon Tool or Special Energy cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Fluffball Star
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Sigilyph

attacks.name: Tri Recharge
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. Attach a number of basic Energy cards up to the number of heads from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Psychic power allows these Pokémon to fly. Some say they were the guardians of an ancient city. Others say they were the guardians' emissaries.

name: Dedenne

attacks.name: Dede-Flash
attacks.text: If your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 60 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Since Dedenne can't generate as much electricity on its own, it steals electricity from outlets or other electric Pokémon.

name: Mimikyu V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dummy Doll
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may prevent all damage done to this Mimikyu V by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon until the end of your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Jealous Eyes
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

name: Mimikyu VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Ominous Numbers
attacks.text: Put 4 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like. If you played Acerola's Premonition from your hand during this turn, place 13 damage counters instead.

attacks.name: Max Shadow
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

name: Milcery

attacks.name: Lead
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: They say that any patisserie visited by Milcery is guaranteed success and good fortune.

name: Alcremie

abilities.name: Additional Order
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your turn does not end when you use Café Master.

attacks.name: Rainbow Flavor
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each type of basic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: When Alcremie is content, the cream it secretes from its hands becomes sweeter and richer.

name: Hitmontop

attacks.name: Spinning Draw
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It launches kicks while spinning. If it spins at high speed, it may bore its way into the ground.

name: Nosepass
flavorText: It hunts without twitching a muscle by pulling in its prey with powerful magnetism. But sometimes it pulls natural enemies in close.

name: Trapinch

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Its nest is a sloped, bowl-like pit in the desert. Once something has fallen in, there is no escape.

name: Vibrava
flavorText: The ultrasonic waves it generates by rubbing its two wings together cause severe headaches.

name: Flygon

attacks.name: Desert Pillar
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: This Pokémon hides in the heart of sandstorms it creates and seldom appears where people can see it.

name: Wormadam

attacks.name: Matron's Anger
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile.

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: When Burmy evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon's body. The cloak is never shed.

name: Riolu
flavorText: It's exceedingly energetic, with enough stamina to keep running all through the night. Taking it for walks can be a challenging experience.

name: Lucario

abilities.name: Roaring Resolve
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may put 2 damage counters on this Pokémon. If you do, search your deck for a Fighting Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Aura Sphere Volley
attacks.text: Discard all Fighting Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 60 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It controls waves known as auras, which are powerful enough to pulverize huge rocks. It uses these waves to take down its prey.

name: Throh
flavorText: It performs throwing moves with first-rate skill. Over the course of many battles, Throh's belt grows darker as it absorbs its wearer's sweat.

name: Sawk

attacks.name: Sweep the Leg
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: If you see a Sawk training in the mountains in its single-minded pursuit of strength, it's best to quietly pass by.

name: Golett
flavorText: They were sculpted from clay in ancient times. No one knows why, but some of them are driven to continually line up boulders.

name: Golurk

attacks.name: Big Hand
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each card in your hand.

flavorText: Artillery platforms built into the walls of ancient castles served as perches from which Golurk could fire energy beams.

name: Grimer

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The wastewater coming from factories is clean these days, so Grimer have nothing to eat. They're said to be on the verge of extinction.

name: Muk

abilities.name: Sludge Street
abilities.text: The Retreat Cost of your opponent's Poisoned Pokémon is Colorless more.

attacks.name: Shrieking Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

flavorText: Because they scatter germs everywhere, they've long been targeted for extermination, leading to a steep decline in their population.

name: Sneasel
flavorText: Its paws conceal sharp claws. If attacked, it suddenly extends the claws and startles its enemy.

name: Weavile

attacks.name: Ransack
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is heads, your opponent reveals their hand. For each heads, choose a card you find there and put it on the bottom of your opponent's deck in any order.

flavorText: They attack their quarry in packs. Prey as large as Mamoswine easily fall to the teamwork of a group of Weavile.

name: Honchkrow V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Boss Pockets
abilities.text: This Pokémon may have up to 4 Pokémon Tools attached to it. If it loses this Ability, discard Pokémon Tools from it until only 1 remains.

attacks.name: Fearsome Shadow
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand.

name: Spiritomb

attacks.name: Ticking Terror
attacks.text: Until the end of your next turn, the Defending Pokémon's Weakness is now Darkness. (The amount of Weakness doesn't change.)

attacks.name: Cursed Drop
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: Exactly 108 spirits gathered to become this Pokémon. Apparently there are some ill-natured spirits in the mix.

name: Purrloin

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It steals things from people just to amuse itself with their frustration. A rivalry exists between this Pokémon and Nickit.

name: Liepard

abilities.name: Trade
abilities.text: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.

flavorText: Don't be fooled by its gorgeous fur and elegant figure. This is a moody and vicious Pokémon.

name: Impidimp

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Through its nose, it sucks in the emanations produced by people and Pokémon when they feel annoyed. It thrives off this negative energy.

name: Morgrem
flavorText: When it gets down on all fours as if to beg for forgiveness, it's trying to lure opponents in so that it can stab them with its spear-like hair.

name: Grimmsnarl

attacks.name: Longhair Shot
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Darkness Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: With the hair wrapped around its body helping to enhance its muscles, this Pokémon can overwhelm even Machamp.

name: Morpeko V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Gnaw and Run
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hangry Spike
attacks.text: If you played Marnie's Pride from your hand during this turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

name: Aggron V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rock Slide
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Merciless Strike
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 150 more damage.

name: Aggron VMAX

attacks.name: Cracking Stomp
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Max Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Wormadam

attacks.name: Matron's Anger
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile.

flavorText: When Burmy evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon's body. The cloak is never shed.

name: Probopass

attacks.name: Magnetic Tension
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Iron Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It uses three small units to catch prey and battle enemies. The main body mostly just gives orders.

name: Heatran

attacks.name: Guard Claw
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Iron Hammer
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Fire Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Boiling blood, like magma, circulates through its body. It makes its dwelling place in volcanic caves.

name: Escavalier

abilities.name: Miraculous Armor
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 100 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon V (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Pike
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: They use shells they've stolen from Shelmet to arm and protect themselves. They're very popular Pokémon in the Galar region.

name: Klink
flavorText: The two minigears that compose this Pokémon are closer than twins. They mesh well only with each other.

name: Klang

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: When Klang goes all out, the minigear links up perfectly with the outer part of the big gear, and this Pokémon's rotation speed increases sharply.

name: Klinklang

abilities.name: Gear Wall
abilities.text: Your Basic Pokémon take 20 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Tumbling Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: From its spikes, it launches powerful blasts of electricity. Its red core contains an enormous amount of energy.

name: Zamazenta V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Regal Stance
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may discard your hand and draw 5 cards. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

attacks.name: Revenge Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

name: Flygon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Draconic Impulse
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon VMAX, this attack does 160 more damage, and discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Gible
flavorText: Gible prefers to stay in narrow holes in the sides of caves heated by geothermal energy. This way, Gible can stay warm even during a blizzard.

name: Gabite
flavorText: This Pokémon emits ultrasonic waves from a protrusion on either side of its head to probe pitch-dark caves.

name: Garchomp

abilities.name: Sonic Slip
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may prevent all damage from and effects of attacks from your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon until the end of your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Dragonblade
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

flavorText: Garchomp makes its home in volcanic mountains. It flies through the sky as fast as a jet airplane, hunting down as much prey as it can.

name: Axew

attacks.name: Ultra Evolution
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Haxorus and put it onto this Axew to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: These Pokémon nest in the ground and use their tusks to crush hard berries. Crushing berries is also how they test each other's strength.

name: Fraxure
flavorText: After battle, this Pokémon carefully sharpens its tusks on river rocks. It needs to take care of its tusks—if one breaks, it will never grow back.

name: Haxorus

attacks.name: Wild Axe
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its resilient tusks are its pride and joy. It licks up dirt to take in the minerals it needs to keep its tusks in top condition.

name: Druddigon

attacks.name: Revenge
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon during their last turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: Druddigon are vicious and cunning. They take up residence in nests dug out by other Pokémon, treating the stolen nests as their own lairs.

name: Dracovish V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Slosh 'n' Crash
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon. If you discarded a Pokémon Tool in this way, this attack does 120 more damage.

attacks.name: Dragon Strike
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Dragon Strike.

name: Farfetch'd

attacks.name: Leek Lash
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The stalk this Pokémon carries in its wings serves as a sword to cut down opponents. In a dire situation, the stalk can also serve as food.

name: Castform

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Hurricane
attacks.text: Move a basic Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its form changes depending on the weather. The rougher conditions get, the rougher Castform's disposition!

name: Starly

attacks.name: Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. Their singing is noisy and annoying.

name: Staravia
flavorText: It lives in forests and fields. Squabbles over territory occur when flocks collide.

name: Staraptor

attacks.name: Strong Breeze
attacks.text: Your opponent shuffles their Active Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck.

attacks.name: Spinning Bird
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.

name: Bidoof
flavorText: It constantly gnaws on logs and rocks to whittle down its front teeth. It nests alongside water.

name: Bibarel

abilities.name: Industrious Incisors
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Tail Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It makes its nest by damming streams with bark and mud. It is known as an industrious worker.

name: Arceus V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Trinity Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon V in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

name: Arceus VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Starbirth
abilities.text: During your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Trinity Nova
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon V in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

name: Minccino

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

name: Cinccino

attacks.name: Triple Axel
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won't tolerate even a speck of the stuff.

name: Tornadus

abilities.name: Sudden Cyclone
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Tornadus expels massive energy from its tail, causing severe storms. Its power is great enough to blow houses away.

name: Hawlucha

attacks.name: Showboating Pose
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Cross-Cut
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It drives its opponents to exhaustion with its agile maneuvers, then ends the fight with a flashy finishing move.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

name: Blunder Policy
rules: If the Pokémon this card is attached to uses an attack, if you flip any coins for the damage or effect of that attack, and if any of them are tails, draw 3 cards at the end of your turn.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Café Master
rules: Choose up to 3 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a different type of basic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Cheren's Care
rules: Put 1 of your Colorless Pokémon that has any damage counters on it and all attached cards into your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Collapsed Stadium
rules: Each player can't have more than 4 Benched Pokémon. If a player has 5 or more Benched Pokémon, they discard Benched Pokémon until they have 4 Pokémon on the Bench. The player who played this card discards first. If more than one effect changes the number of Benched Pokémon allowed, use the smaller number.

name: Cynthia's Ambition
rules: Draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand. If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn, draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand instead.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Fresh Water Set
rules: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
