name: Flareon

abilities.name: Incandescent Awakening
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has a Memory Capsule attached, Grass Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities.

flavorText: Once it has stored up enough heat, this Pokémon's body temperature can reach up to 1,700 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Vaporeon

abilities.name: Torrential Awakening
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has a Memory Capsule attached, Fire Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities.

flavorText: When Vaporeon's fins begin to vibrate, it is a sign that rain will come within a few hours.

name: Octillery

abilities.name: Rapid Strike Search
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Rapid Strike card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. You can't use more than 1 Rapid Strike Search Ability each turn.

flavorText: It traps enemies with its suction-cupped tentacles, then smashes them with its rock-hard head.

name: Jolteon

abilities.name: Thunderous Awakening
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has a Memory Capsule attached, Water Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities.

flavorText: If it is angered or startled, the fur all over its body bristles like sharp needles that pierce foes.

name: Zekrom

attacks.name: Wild Shock
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When the interior part of its tail spins like a motor, Zekrom can generate many bolts of lightning to blast its surroundings.

name: Dusknoir

abilities.name: Spectral Breach
abilities.text: All Special Energy attached to Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) provide Colorless Energy and have no other effect.

flavorText: At the bidding of transmissions from the spirit world, it steals people and Pokémon away. No one knows whether it has a will of its own.

name: Dedenne

attacks.name: Dede-Flash
attacks.text: If your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 60 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Since Dedenne can't generate as much electricity on its own, it steals electricity from outlets or other electric Pokémon.

name: Alcremie

abilities.name: Additional Order
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your turn does not end when you use Café Master.

attacks.name: Rainbow Flavor
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each type of basic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: When Alcremie is content, the cream it secretes from its hands becomes sweeter and richer.

name: Ariados

abilities.name: Spider Net
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Evolution Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It spews threads from its mouth to catch its prey. When night falls, it leaves its web to go hunt aggressively.

name: Houndoom

abilities.name: Single Strike Roar
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Single Strike Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Single Strike Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

flavorText: Identifiable by its eerie howls, people a long time ago thought it was the grim reaper and feared it.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Vee-Search
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon V, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

name: Oranguru

abilities.name: Primate Wisdom
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch a card from your hand with the top card of your deck.

flavorText: It knows the forest inside and out. If it comes across a wounded Pokémon, Oranguru will gather medicinal herbs to treat it.

name: Boltund V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Electrify
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Bolt Storm
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Lightning Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

name: Sylveon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dream Gift
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

name: Sylveon VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Precious Touch
attacks.text: Attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, heal 120 damage from that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Max Harmony
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each different type of Pokémon on your Bench.

name: Mimikyu V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dummy Doll
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may prevent all damage done to this Mimikyu V by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon until the end of your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Jealous Eyes
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

name: Mimikyu VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Ominous Numbers
attacks.text: Put 4 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like. If you played Acerola's Premonition from your hand during this turn, place 13 damage counters instead.

attacks.name: Max Shadow
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

name: Single Strike Urshifu V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Laser Focus
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Fighting Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Impact Blow
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Impact Blow.

name: Single Strike Urshifu VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max One Blow
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Rapid Strike Urshifu V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Rapid Strike Urshifu VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Gale Thrust
attacks.text: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

attacks.name: G-Max Rapid Flow
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Umbreon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mean Look
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Moonlight Blade
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Umbreon VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dark Signal
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

name: Acerola's Premonition
rules: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you draw a card for each Trainer card you find there.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Café Master
rules: Choose up to 3 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a different type of basic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Gloria
rules: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon that don't have a Rule Box and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck. (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Rapid Strike Style Mustard
rules: You can play this card only when it is the last card in your hand.
Put a Rapid Strike Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench. If you do, draw 5 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Single Strike Style Mustard
rules: You can play this card only when it is the last card in your hand.
Search your deck for a Single Strike Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck. If you searched your deck in this way, draw 5 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Single Strike Urshifu VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max One Blow
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Rapid Strike Urshifu VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Gale Thrust
attacks.text: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

attacks.name: G-Max Rapid Flow
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Moltres

attacks.name: Inferno Wings
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 70 more. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

flavorText: It's one of the legendary bird Pokémon. When Moltres flaps its flaming wings, they glimmer with a dazzling red glow.

name: Lucario

abilities.name: Roaring Resolve
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may put 2 damage counters on this Pokémon. If you do, search your deck for a Fighting Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Aura Sphere Volley
attacks.text: Discard all Fighting Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 60 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It controls waves known as auras, which are powerful enough to pulverize huge rocks. It uses these waves to take down its prey.

name: Liepard

abilities.name: Trade
abilities.text: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.

flavorText: Don't be fooled by its gorgeous fur and elegant figure. This is a moody and vicious Pokémon.

name: Bibarel

abilities.name: Industrious Incisors
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Tail Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It makes its nest by damming streams with bark and mud. It is known as an industrious worker.

name: Flapple

attacks.name: Flight Up
attacks.text: Attach up to 3 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Corrosive Acid
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It flies on wings of apple skin and spits a powerful acid. It can also change its shape into that of an apple.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Be Prepared
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

name: Leafeon

attacks.name: Leaf Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Grass Knot
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: This Pokémon's tail is blade sharp, with a fantastic cutting edge that can slice right though large trees.

name: Glaceon

attacks.name: Hail
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Frosty Typhoon
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Frosty Typhoon.

flavorText: The coldness emanating from Glaceon causes powdery snow to form, making it quite a popular Pokémon at ski resorts.

name: Galarian Obstagoon

attacks.name: Rampaging Kick
attacks.text: Discard 2 Darkness Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.

name: Pikachu V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Lightning Blast
attacks.text: You may discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 120 more damage.

name: Lycanroc V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crashing Fangs
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Corviknight V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Sky Hurricane
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Sky Hurricane.

name: Espeon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Zen Shot
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon V. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Sylveon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dream Gift
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

name: Umbreon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mean Look
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Moonlight Blade
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Arceus V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Trinity Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon V in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

name: Lucario V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crushing Punch
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Lucario VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fighting Knuckle
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 120 more damage.

attacks.name: Aura Star
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Morpeko V-UNION
rules: How to play a Pokémon V-UNION: Once per game during your turn, combine 4 different Morpeko V-UNION from your discard pile and put them onto your Bench.
V-UNION rule: When your Pokémon V-UNION is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Union Gain
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Lightning Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: All You Can Eat
attacks.text: Draw cards until you have 10 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Burst Wheel
attacks.text: Discard all energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Morpeko V-UNION
rules: How to play a Pokémon V-UNION: Once per game during your turn, combine 4 different Morpeko V-UNION from your discard pile and put them onto your Bench.
V-UNION rule: When your Pokémon V-UNION is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Union Gain
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Lightning Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: All You Can Eat
attacks.text: Draw cards until you have 10 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Burst Wheel
attacks.text: Discard all energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Morpeko V-UNION
rules: How to play a Pokémon V-UNION: Once per game during your turn, combine 4 different Morpeko V-UNION from your discard pile and put them onto your Bench.
V-UNION rule: When your Pokémon V-UNION is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Union Gain
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Lightning Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: All You Can Eat
attacks.text: Draw cards until you have 10 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Burst Wheel
attacks.text: Discard all energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Morpeko V-UNION
rules: How to play a Pokémon V-UNION: Once per game during your turn, combine 4 different Morpeko V-UNION from your discard pile and put them onto your Bench.
V-UNION rule: When your Pokémon V-UNION is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Union Gain
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Lightning Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: All You Can Eat
attacks.text: Draw cards until you have 10 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Burst Wheel
attacks.text: Discard all energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Boltund V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Smash Turn
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Electrobullet
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Rowlet

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Flies noiselessly on delicate wings. It has mastered the art of deftly launching dagger-sharp feathers from those same wings.

name: Cyndaquil

attacks.name: Charge Energy
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Hails from the Johto region. Though usually curled into a ball due to its timid disposition, it harbors tremendous firepower.

name: Oshawott
flavorText: This Pokémon from the Unova region uses the shell on its belly as a weapon to cut down its foes. Thus, I've conferred upon this shell the name "scalchop."

name: Beedrill V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Twineedle
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Swarming Sting
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each of your Beedrill V in play. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Hisuian Voltorb

attacks.name: Cheerful Charge
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only during your first turn. Choose up to 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: An enigmatic Pokémon that happens to bear a resemblance to a Poké Ball. When excited, it discharges the electric current it has stored in its belly, then lets out a great, uproarious laugh.

name: Hisuian Electrode

attacks.name: Triple Draw
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

flavorText: The tissue on the surface of its body is curiously similar in composition to an Apricorn. When irritated, this Pokémon lets loose an electric current equal to 20 lightning bolts.

name: Scyther
flavorText: The large, wickedly sharp scythes on its forearms are truly fearsome weapons. Prey's attempts to flee are unfailingly thwarted by this Pokémon's nimble motions.

name: Scyther

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The large, wickedly sharp scythes on its forearms are truly fearsome weapons. Prey's attempts to flee are unfailingly thwarted by this Pokémon's nimble motions.

name: Yanma
flavorText: Its frail wings are so thin that one can see clear through them. However, during flight these wings exhibit the power to churn air with force enough to launch a house skyward.

name: Yanmega

attacks.name: Wide Wing
attacks.text: If you have more cards in your hand than your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.

name: Heracross

attacks.name: Overhead Throw
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This Pokémon has an unparalleled horn. Heracross itself demonstrates tremendous power—it's capable of throwing several people trained in the traditional arts of war at once.

name: Kricketot

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: When its antennae hit each other, it sounds like the music of a xylophone.

name: Kricketune

abilities.name: Swelling Tune
abilities.text: Your Grass Pokémon in play, except any Kricketune, get +40 HP. You can't apply more than 1 Swelling Tune Ability at a time.

flavorText: It uses its cutlass-like arms to produce sound, the melody of which varies from individual to individual. It is a worthwhile endeavor to seek out one's favorite tunes.

name: Combee

attacks.name: Honey Courier
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: The members of the trio spend all their time together. Each one has a slightly different taste in nectar.

name: Vespiquen

attacks.name: Honey Rush
attacks.text: Reveal any number of Sweet Honey cards from your hand. This attack does 60 damage for each card you revealed in this way.

flavorText: It skillfully commands its grubs in battles with its enemies. The grubs are willing to risk their lives to defend Vespiquen.

name: Leafeon

attacks.name: Energy Garden
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards of different types and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Leafy Cyclone
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Cells similar to those of plants have been found in its fur. Its hard tail can fell a large tree with one stroke, and the tail's sharpness exceeds even that of a sword crafted by a master.

name: Shaymin

attacks.name: Encouraging Gift
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only during your first turn. Search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: When the turning of seasons brings the cruel winter to its end and the joyous people give thanks to the heavens, Shaymin appears and covers the withered land with flowers.

name: Petilil

attacks.name: Spin Turn
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The leaves on its head are highly valued for medicinal purposes. Dry the leaves in the sun, boil them, and then drink the bitter decoction for remarkably effective relief from fatigue.

name: Hisuian Lilligant

attacks.name: Twister Lutz
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: I suspect that its well-developed legs are the result of a life spent on mountains covered in deep snow. The scent it exudes from its flower crown heartens those in proximity.

name: Hisuian Lilligant V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dance Gracefully
attacks.text: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

name: Hisuian Lilligant VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Star Perfume
abilities.text: During your turn, you may search your deck for up to 5 in any combination of Grass Pokémon and Grass Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Parallel Spin
attacks.text: You may put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand. If you do, this attack does 100 more damage.

name: Rowlet

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Flies noiselessly on delicate wings. It has mastered the art of deftly launching dagger-sharp feathers from those same wings.

name: Dartrix
flavorText: Regularly basks in sunlight to gather power—presumably due to the frigid climate. Nonetheless, the edges of the blade quills set into its wings are keen as ever.

name: Ponyta
flavorText: These Pokémon live in herds out in the grassland. Newborn foals lack their fiery manes, which will develop about an hour after birth.

name: Rapidash

attacks.name: Ring of Fire
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.

name: Cyndaquil

attacks.name: Charge Energy
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Hails from the Johto region. Though usually curled into a ball due to its timid disposition, it harbors tremendous firepower.

name: Quilava
flavorText: This creature's fur is most mysterious—it is wholly impervious to the burning touch of flame. Should Quilava turn its back to you, take heed! Such a posture indicates a forthcoming attack.

name: Heatran V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Heat Burn
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Magma Fall
attacks.text: If you have a Stadium in play, this attack does 90 more damage.

name: Heatran VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Magma Gain
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if you have a Stadium in play, you may heal 50 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Max Heat Burst
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Radiant Heatran
rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.

attacks.name: Raging Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: Boiling blood, like magma, circulates through its body. It makes its dwelling place in volcanic caves.

name: Psyduck

attacks.name: Spacing Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Suffers perpetual headaches. If the agony grows too great, Psyduck's latent power erupts, contrary to Psyduck's intent. Ergo, I am exploring ways to ease the pain.

name: Golduck

attacks.name: Entangled Dive
attacks.text: Discard each player's Active Pokémon and all attached cards. (You choose a new Active Pokémon first.)

flavorText: This Pokémon lives in gently flowing rivers. It paddles through the water with its long limbs, putting its graceful swimming skills on display.

name: Starmie V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Energy Spiral
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

name: Swinub

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Swinub excels at sniffing out mushrooms buried beneath grass or snow. Since ancient times, the people of Hisui have often relied upon this skill.

name: Piloswine

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: If it charges at an enemy, the hairs on its back stand up straight. It is very sensitive to sound.

name: Mamoswine

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Iceberg Press
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: This Pokémon can be spotted in wall paintings from as far back as 10,000 years ago. For a while, it was thought to have gone extinct.

name: Mantine

attacks.name: Borne Ashore
attacks.text: Put a Basic Pokémon from either player's discard pile onto that player's Bench.

flavorText: This calm and gentle Pokémon swims gracefully through the sea. After building speed, it can leap out of the water. It is often misidentified as a bird Pokémon due to this behavior.

name: Barboach
flavorText: Makes its home in swamps with murky water. The poor visibility hides this Pokémon from predators, and the slime on its body makes grasping it difficult.

name: Whiscash

attacks.name: Thrash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It makes its nest at the bottom of swamps. It will eat anything—if it is alive, Whiscash will eat it.

name: Regice

attacks.name: Regi Gate
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Blizzard Bind
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon V, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: With cold air that can reach temperatures as low as −328 degrees Fahrenheit, Regice instantly freezes any creature that approaches it.

name: Glaceon

attacks.name: Frost Wall
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Evolution Pokémon.

flavorText: Glaceon is able to lower its body temperature very quickly. It freezes the atmosphere, creating diamond dust that glitters like gems while it flutters and dances around.

name: Origin Forme Palkia V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rule the Region
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Stadium card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Hydro Break
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Origin Forme Palkia VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Star Portal
abilities.text: During your turn, you may attach up to 3 Water Energy cards from your discard pile to your Water Pokémon in any way you like. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Subspace Swell
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

name: Oshawott
flavorText: This Pokémon from the Unova region uses the shell on its belly as a weapon to cut down its foes. Thus, I've conferred upon this shell the name "scalchop.".

name: Dewott

attacks.name: Razor Shell
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its exquisite double-scalchop technique is likely the result of daily training, and it can send even masters of the blade fleeing in defeat.

name: Hisuian Basculin

attacks.name: Gather the Crew
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Though it differs from other Basculin in several respects, including demeanor—this one is gentle—I have categorized it as a regional form given the vast array of shared qualities.

name: Hisuian Basculegion

attacks.name: Grudge Dive
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon during their last turn, this attack does 90 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Clads itself in the souls of comrades that perished before fulfilling their goals of journeying upstream. No other species throughout all Hisui's rivers is Basculegion's equal.

name: Keldeo

attacks.name: Line Force
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: When it is resolute, its body fills with power and it becomes swifter. Its jumps are then too fast to follow.

name: Radiant Greninja
rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.

abilities.name: Concealed Cards
abilities.text: You must discard an Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Moonlight Shuriken
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 90 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It appears and vanishes with a ninja's grace. It toys with its enemies using swift movements, while slicing them with throwing stars of sharpest water.

name: Bergmite
flavorText: Lives on mountains blanketed in perennial snow. It freezes water vapor in the air to make the ice helmet that it dons for defense.

name: Hisuian Avalugg

abilities.name: Massive Ice
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Mountain Gale
attacks.text: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 120 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium.

flavorText: The armor of ice covering its lower jaw puts steel to shame and can shatter rocks with ease. This Pokémon barrels along steep mountain paths, cleaving through the deep snow.

name: Galarian Mr. Rime V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Surprising Hand
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Item cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Customized Cane
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 90 more damage.

name: Luxray V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fang Snipe
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard a Trainer card you find there.

attacks.name: Radiating Pulse
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Regieleki

attacks.name: Electromagnetic Sonar
attacks.text: Put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Targeted Bolt
attacks.text: Discard 2 Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This Pokémon is a cluster of electrical energy. It's said that removing the rings on Regieleki's body will unleash the Pokémon's latent power.

name: Hisuian Typhlosion

abilities.name: Supernatural Orb
abilities.text: You must discard a Psychic Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Burned and Confused.

attacks.name: Shadow Bind
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Said to purify lost, forsaken souls with its flames and guide them to the afterlife. I believe its form has been influenced by the energy of the sacred mountain towering at Hisui's center.

name: Hisuian Typhlosion V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Petrifying Flame
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

name: Hisuian Typhlosion VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hollow Flame
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Shimmering Star
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has exactly 4 damage counters on it, that Pokémon is Knocked Out. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Togepi

abilities.name: Touch of Happiness
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may heal 10 damage from your Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This ovate creature is frequently mistaken for a moving egg when encountered out in the fields or in the mountains. Its guileless smile soothes the soul.

name: Togetic

abilities.name: Voice of Happiness
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may heal 30 damage from your Active Pokémon.

flavorText: They say that it will appear before kindhearted, caring people and shower them with happiness.

name: Togekiss

abilities.name: Shine of Happiness
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may heal 90 damage from your Active Pokémon.

flavorText: These Pokémon are never seen anywhere near conflict or turmoil. In recent times, they've hardly been seen at all.

name: Misdreavus
flavorText: It conceals itself in darkness, sending chills up travelers' spines with its childlike weeping. As it observes the frightened travelers with glee, the red orbs upon its chest let off an eerie light.

name: Mismagius

attacks.name: Ominous Prose
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. If they have 4 or more cards in their hand, choose all but 3, and your opponent shuffles the chosen cards into their deck.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its muttered curses can cause awful headaches or terrifying visions that torment others.

name: Ralts

attacks.name: Teleportation Burst
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Tends to prefer people with a chipper disposition to those who are gloomy, but it has shown no discrimination with regard to age or gender. Needs more research.

name: Kirlia

attacks.name: Teleportation Burst
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: If its Trainer becomes happy, it overflows with energy, dancing joyously while spinning about.

name: Gallade

abilities.name: Buddy Catch
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Swirling Slice
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: True to its honorable-warrior image, it uses the blades on its elbows only in defense of something or someone.

name: Drifloon

attacks.name: Triple Spin
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Said to lure away young children and carry them off to the afterlife. Some whisper that Drifloon are formed of reincarnated human souls, but these rumors are as yet unconfirmed.

name: Drifblim

attacks.name: Spooky Balloon
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Some say this Pokémon is a collection of souls burdened with regrets, silently drifting through the dusk.

name: Uxie

attacks.name: Wise Guidance
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It is said that its emergence gave humans the intelligence to improve their quality of life.

name: Mesprit

abilities.name: Mental Shroud
abilities.text: If you have Uxie and Azelf in play, each of your Pokémon has no Weakness.

flavorText: It sleeps at the bottom of a lake. Its spirit is said to leave its body to fly on the lake's surface.

name: Azelf

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It is thought that Uxie, Mesprit, and Azelf all came from the same egg.

name: Diancie

abilities.name: Princess's Curtain
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent plays a Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to your Benched Basic Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spike Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: It can instantly create many diamonds by compressing the carbon in the air between its hands.

name: Wyrdeer

abilities.name: Hurried Gait
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw a card.

attacks.name: Extrasensory
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: The black orbs shine with an uncanny light when the Pokémon is erecting invisible barriers. The fur shed from its beard retains heat well and is a highly useful material for winter clothing.

name: Hisuian Growlithe

attacks.name: Defensive Posture
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

flavorText: They patrol their territory in pairs. I believe the igneous rock components in the fur of this species are the result of volcanic activity in its habitat.

name: Hisuian Arcanine

attacks.name: Scorching Horn
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Fire Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Snaps at its foes with fangs cloaked in blazing flame. Despite its bulk, it deftly feints every which way, leading opponents on a deceptively merry chase as it all but dances around them.

name: Machamp V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Revenge Buster
attacks.text: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 50 more damage.

name: Machamp VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Revenge Buster
attacks.text: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 140 more damage.

attacks.name: G-Max Chi Strike
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use G-Max Chi Strike.

name: Sudowoodo

abilities.name: Vamoose
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, it has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Though it pretends to be a tree, it fails to fool even children. To the touch, its body feels more like rock than tree bark. Sudowoodo's extreme aversion to water merits special note.

name: Regirock

attacks.name: Regi Gate
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Every bit of Regirock's body is made of stone. As parts of its body erode, this Pokémon sticks rocks to itself to repair what's been lost.

name: Cranidos

attacks.name: Stone Edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Its hard skull is its distinguishing feature. It snapped trees by headbutting them, and then it fed on their ripe berries.

name: Rampardos

attacks.name: Jurassic Hammer
attacks.text: If your opponent has 3 or fewer cards in their hand, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This ancient Pokémon used headbutts skillfully. Its brain was really small, so some theories suggest that its stupidity led to its extinction.

name: Lucario V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crushing Punch
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Hippopotas
flavorText: Though large and languid, Hippopotas is difficult to detect due to its tendency to burrow into and lurk beneath the soil. When agitated or excited, it expels sand from its nostrils.

name: Hippowdon

attacks.name: Sand Breath
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Stones can get stuck in the ports on their bodies. Dwebble help dislodge such stones, so Hippowdon look after these Pokémon.

name: Radiant Hawlucha
rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.

abilities.name: Big Match
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, your Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon VMAX (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It drives its opponents to exhaustion with its agile maneuvers, then ends the fight with a flashy finishing move.

name: Hisuian Decidueye

attacks.name: Piercing Claw
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Direct Arrow
attacks.text: This attack does 80 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The air stored inside the rachises of Decidueye's feathers insulates the Pokémon against Hisui's extreme cold. This is firm proof that evolution can be influenced by environment.

name: Hisuian Decidueye V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mountain Hunt
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Close-Quarters Shooting
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Hisuian Decidueye VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Star of Fortune
abilities.text: During your turn, you may draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Somersault Feathers
attacks.text: You may discard up to 3 Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 30 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Kleavor

attacks.name: Rout
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rocky Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: A violent creature that fells towering trees with its crude axes and shields itself with hard stone. If one should chance upon this Pokémon in the wilds, one's only recourse is to flee.

name: Kleavor

attacks.name: Timber Cleave
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Berserker Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: A violent creature that fells towering trees with its crude axes and shields itself with hard stone. If one should chance upon this Pokémon in the wilds, one's only recourse is to flee.

name: Kleavor V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Axe Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Hisuian Qwilfish
flavorText: Fishers detest this troublesome Pokémon because it sprays poison from its spines, getting it everywhere. A different form of Qwilfish lives in other regions.

name: Hisuian Qwilfish

attacks.name: Spiny Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Fishers detest this troublesome Pokémon because it sprays poison from its spines, getting it everywhere. A different form of Qwilfish lives in other regions.

name: Hisuian Overqwil

attacks.name: Tormenting Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 5 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.

attacks.name: Pinning
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Its lancelike spikes and savage temperament have earned it the nickname "sea fiend." It slurps up poison to nourish itself.

name: Hisuian Overqwil

attacks.name: Dirty Press
attacks.text: If you have at least 3 Darkness Energy in play, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Its lancelike spikes and savage temperament have earned it the nickname "sea fiend." It slurps up poison to nourish itself.

name: Hisuian Sneasel
flavorText: Its sturdy, curved claws are ideal for traversing precipitous cliffs. From the tips of these claws drips a venom that infiltrates the nerves of any prey caught in Sneasel's grasp.

name: Hisuian Sneasler

abilities.name: Carry and Climb
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, your Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost is ColorlessColorless less.

flavorText: Because of Sneasler's virulent poison and daunting physical prowess, no other species could hope to best it on the frozen highlands. Preferring solitude, this species does not form packs.

name: Hisuian Sneasler V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Poison Claws
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Dire Claw
attacks.text: This attack does 80 damage for each Special Condition affecting your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Poochyena

attacks.name: Slight Intrusion
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It has a very tenacious nature. Its acute sense of smell lets it chase a chosen prey without ever losing track.

name: Mightyena

abilities.name: Hustle Bark
abilities.text: If your opponent has any Pokémon VMAX in play, this Pokémon's attacks cost ColorlessColorlessColorless less.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: It chases down prey in a pack of around ten. They defeat foes with perfectly coordinated teamwork.

name: Absol

attacks.name: Swirling Disaster
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Claw Rend
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Swift as the wind, Absol races through fields and mountains. Its curved, bow-like horn is acutely sensitive to the warning signs of natural disasters.

name: Darkrai V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dark Void
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Darkrai VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Star Abyss
abilities.text: During your turn, you may put up to 2 Item cards from your discard pile into your hand. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Dark Pulse
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Darkness Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

name: Hisuian Samurott

abilities.name: Wily Stance
abilities.text: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Dark Mastery
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Hard of heart and deft of blade, this rare form of Samurott is a product of the Pokémon's evolution in the region of Hisui. Its turbulent blows crash into foes like ceaseless pounding waves.

name: Hisuian Samurott V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Basket Crash
attacks.text: Discard up to 2 Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Hisuian Samurott VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Moon Cleave Star
abilities.text: During your turn, you may put 4 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Merciless Blade
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 110 more damage.

name: Nickit
flavorText: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

name: Thievul

abilities.name: Baffling
abilities.text: If your opponent has 2 or fewer Prize cards remaining, whenever your opponent plays a Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to your Benched Pokémon V.

flavorText: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Magnetic Catch
attacks.text: Shuffle up to 3 Metal Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

flavorText: A bizarre Pokémon with but a single eye embedded in an iron sphere. I suspect this creature levitates due to the magnetism it emits from its arms, which resemble horseshoe-shaped magnets.

name: Magneton

attacks.name: Bounce Back
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon is three Magnemite that have linked together. Magneton sends out powerful radio waves to study its surroundings.

name: Magnezone

abilities.name: Giga Magnet
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck and attach any number of Metal Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: Some say that Magnezone receives signals from space via the antenna on its head and that it's being controlled by some mysterious being.

name: Registeel

attacks.name: Regi Gate
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Heavy Slam
attacks.text: This attack does 50 less damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: Registeel's body is made of a strange material that is flexible enough to stretch and shrink but also more durable than any metal.

name: Shieldon

attacks.name: Hard Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Although its fossils can be found in layers of primeval rock, nothing but its face has ever been discovered.

name: Bastiodon

abilities.name: Primal Fortress
abilities.text: Your Pokémon take 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon V (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Iron Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon is from roughly 100 million years ago. Its terrifyingly tough face is harder than steel.

name: Bronzor

attacks.name: Metal Press
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Floats using a mysterious energy. The pattern engraved upon its back is held as sacred and can sometimes be found in imagery from ancient cemeteries and other such timeworn places.

name: Bronzong

abilities.name: Heatproof
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Fire Pokémon.

flavorText: Some believe it to be a deity that summons rain clouds. When angered, it lets out a warning cry that rings out like the tolling of a bell.

name: Origin Forme Dialga V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Metal Coating
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Metal Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

name: Origin Forme Dialga VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Metal Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Star Chronos
attacks.text: Take another turn after this one. (Skip Pokémon Checkup.) (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Pawniard

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It uses river stones to maintain the cutting edges of the blades covering its body. These sharpened blades allow it to bring down opponents.

name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Vengeful Cut
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched Pawniard.

flavorText: It's accompanied by a large retinue of Pawniard. Bisharp keeps a keen eye on its minions, ensuring none of them even think of double-crossing it.

name: Garchomp V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sonic Strike
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 220 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Regidrago

abilities.name: Dragon's Hoard
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may draw cards until you have 4 cards in your hand. You can't use more than 1 Dragon's Hoard Ability each turn.

flavorText: An academic theory proposes that Regidrago's arms were once the head of an ancient dragon Pokémon. The theory remains unproven.

name: Eevee

abilities.name: Resonant Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play a Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your other Eevee, you may search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.

name: Hoothoot

abilities.name: Stand Sentry
abilities.text: Basic Energy attached to your Benched Pokémon can't be discarded by an effect of your opponent's Item or Supporter cards.

flavorText: It always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.

name: Noctowl

attacks.name: Silent Wing
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its eyes are specially developed to enable it to see clearly even in murky darkness and minimal light.

name: Teddiursa

attacks.name: Gather Food
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put an Item card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: It licks its paws because of the sweet honey that has soaked into them. It is cunning, stealing into the nests of Combee and taking for itself the honey that the Combee have amassed.

name: Ursaring

attacks.name: Continuous Slap
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: When the cold season arrives in Hisui, this Pokémon will wander fields and mountains alike in search of its favorite berries. Ursaring's hunger during this time makes it a ferocious danger.

name: Ursaluna

attacks.name: Peat Hunt
attacks.text: Put up to 2 cards from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Bulky Bump
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: I believe it was Hisui's swampy terrain that gave Ursaluna its burly physique and newfound capacity to manipulate peat at will.

name: Stantler

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its strangely shaped antlers have the power to bewitch those who see them. Medicine made by grinding up the black orbs from fallen antlers is an effective treatment for insomnia.

name: Miltank

abilities.name: Miracle Body
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon V.

attacks.name: Rout
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Miltank produces highly nutritious milk, so it's been supporting the lives of people and other Pokémon since ancient times.

name: Glameow

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Bewitches humans with its helical tail and piercing gaze. Its hidden claws are quite sharp as well, making this Pokémon an exceedingly tricky opponent if antagonized.

name: Purugly

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: To make itself appear intimidatingly beefy, it tightly cinches its waist with its twin tails.

name: Chatot

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Jabber On
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: A versatile performer skilled in the imitation of human speech. It is said that older, more experienced Chatot can even understand the meaning of the words they mimic.

name: Regigigas

abilities.name: Ancient Wisdom
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if you have Regirock, Regice, Registeel, Regieleki, and Regidrago in play, you may attach up to 3 Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gigaton Break
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon VMAX, this attack does 150 more damage.

flavorText: There is an enduring legend that states this Pokémon towed continents with ropes.

name: Rufflet

attacks.name: Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its chick-like looks belie its hotheadedness. It challenges its parents at every opportunity, desperate to prove its strength.

name: Hisuian Braviary

attacks.name: Psywave
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Slashing Strike

name: Abomasnow

abilities.name: Toughness Boost
abilities.text: Your Single Strike Pokémon in play, except any Abomasnow, get +50 HP. You can't apply more than 1 Toughness Boost Ability at a time.

flavorText: The Pokémon is known to bring blizzards. A shake of its massive body is enough to cause whiteout conditions.

name: Flapple

abilities.name: Apple Drop
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. If you placed any damage counters in this way, shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

attacks.name: Acid Spray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It ate a sour apple, and that induced its evolution. In its cheeks, it stores an acid capable of causing chemical burns.

name: Kingdra

abilities.name: Deep Sea King
abilities.text: When your Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, you may move any amount of Water Energy from that Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aqua Burst
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It stores energy by sleeping at underwater depths at which no other life forms can survive.

name: Frosmoth

abilities.name: Ice Dance
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Water Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Water Pokémon.

flavorText: It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.

name: Gardevoir

abilities.name: Shining Arcana
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck and attach any number of basic Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Put the other cards into your hand.

attacks.name: Brainwave
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: To protect its Trainer, it will expend all its psychic power to create a small black hole.

name: Wyrdeer

abilities.name: Hurried Gait
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw a card.

attacks.name: Extrasensory
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: The black orbs shine with an uncanny light when the Pokémon is erecting invisible barriers. The fur shed from its beard retains heat well and is a highly useful material for winter clothing.

name: Falinks

attacks.name: Rapid Strike Squad
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Rapid Strike Pokémon in play.

flavorText: The six of them work together as one Pokémon. Teamwork is also their battle strategy, and they constantly change their formation as they fight.

name: Kleavor

attacks.name: Timber Cleave
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Berserker Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: A violent creature that fells towering trees with its crude axes and shields itself with hard stone. If one should chance upon this Pokémon in the wilds, one's only recourse is to flee.

name: Mightyena

abilities.name: Hustle Bark
abilities.text: If your opponent has any Pokémon VMAX in play, this Pokémon's attacks cost ColorlessColorlessColorless less.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: It chases down prey in a pack of around ten. They defeat foes with perfectly coordinated teamwork.

name: Galarian Obstagoon

abilities.name: Untamed Shout

attacks.name: Obstruct
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

flavorText: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.

name: Bronzong

abilities.name: Metal Transfer
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a Metal Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Many scientists suspect that this Pokémon originated outside the Galar region, based on the patterns on its body.

flavorText: It always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.

name: Galarian Articuno V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Reconstitute
abilities.text: You must discard 2 cards from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw a card.

attacks.name: Psyray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Shadow Rider Calyrex V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shadow Mist
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Special Energy or Stadium cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Astral Barrage
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon and put 5 damage counters on each of them.

name: Shadow Rider Calyrex VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Underworld Door
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Psychic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Psychic Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Max Geist
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

name: Galarian Zapdos V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Fighting Instinct
abilities.text: This Pokémon's attacks cost Colorless less for each of your opponent's Pokémon V in play.

attacks.name: Thunderous Kick
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Galarian Moltres V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Direflame Wings
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Darkness Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon. You can't use more than 1 Direflame Wings Ability each turn.

attacks.name: Aura Burn
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Zacian V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Intrepid Sword
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 3 cards of your deck and attach any number of Metal Energy cards you find there to this Pokémon. Put the other cards into your hand. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

attacks.name: Brave Blade
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Zamazenta V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dauntless Shield
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon VMAX.

attacks.name: Assault Tackle
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Garchomp V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sonic Strike
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 220 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Allister
rules: Draw 3 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, discard up to 3 cards from your hand. (You must discard at least 1 card.)
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Bea
rules: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck, and attach any Energy cards you discarded in this way to your Benched Fighting Pokémon in any way you like.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Melony
rules: Attach a Water Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon V. If you do, draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Milo
rules: Discard up to 2 cards from your hand, and draw 2 cards for each card you discarded in this way.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Piers
rules: Search your deck for an Energy card and a Darkness Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Ice Rider Calyrex VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Ride of the High King
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Max Lance
attacks.text: You may discard up to 2 Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 120 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Shadow Rider Calyrex VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Underworld Door
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Psychic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Psychic Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Max Geist
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

name: Bulbasaur
flavorText: There is a plant seed on its back right from the day this Pokémon is born. The seed slowly grows larger.

name: Ivysaur

attacks.name: Summoning Aroma
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: When the bulb on its back grows large, it appears to lose the ability to stand on its hind legs.

name: Venusaur

abilities.name: Loopy Lasso
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon, and the new Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

flavorText: Its plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

name: Radiant Venusaur
rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.

abilities.name: Sunny Bloom
abilities.text: Once at the end of your turn (after your attack), you may use this Ability. Draw cards until you have 4 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Pollen Hazard
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.

flavorText: Its plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

name: Alolan Exeggutor V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Growing Tall
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 5 Grass Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Head Swing
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Spinarak

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Although the poison from its fangs isn't that strong, it's potent enough to weaken prey that gets caught in its web.

name: Ariados

attacks.name: Poison String-Up
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 50 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It spews threads from its mouth to catch its prey. When night falls, it leaves its web to go hunt aggressively.

name: Charmander

attacks.name: Tail on Fire
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It has a preference for hot things. When it rains, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

name: Charmeleon

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It has a barbaric nature. In battle, it whips its fiery tail around and slashes away with sharp claws.

name: Charizard

abilities.name: Burn Brightly
abilities.text: Each basic Fire Energy attached to your Pokémon provides FireFire Energy. You can't apply more than 1 Burn Brightly Ability at a time.

attacks.name: Flare Blitz
attacks.text: Discard all Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. It may cause forest fires by blowing flames.

name: Radiant Charizard
rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.

abilities.name: Excited Heart
abilities.text: This Pokémon's attacks cost Colorless less for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

attacks.name: Combustion Blast
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Combustion Blast.

flavorText: It spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. It may cause forest fires by blowing flames.

name: Moltres

abilities.name: Flare Symbol
abilities.text: Your Basic Fire Pokémon's attacks, except any Moltres, do 10 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It's one of the legendary bird Pokémon. When Moltres flaps its flaming wings, they glimmer with a dazzling red glow.

name: Numel

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: The magma in its body reaches 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit. Its hump gets smaller when it uses Fire-type moves.

name: Camerupt

attacks.name: Split Bomb
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: If angered, the humps on its back erupt in a shower of molten lava. It lives in the craters of volcanoes.

name: Squirtle
flavorText: When it retracts its long neck into its shell, it squirts out water with vigorous force.

name: Wartortle

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is recognized as a symbol of longevity. If its shell has algae on it, that Wartortle is very old.

name: Blastoise

abilities.name: Vitality Spring
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 6 Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It crushes its foe under its heavy body to cause fainting. In a pinch, it will withdraw inside its shell.

name: Radiant Blastoise
rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.

abilities.name: Pump Shot
abilities.text: You must discard a Water Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Torrential Cannon
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Torrential Cannon.

flavorText: It crushes its foe under its heavy body to cause fainting. In a pinch, it will withdraw inside its shell.

name: Slowpoke

attacks.name: Hold Still
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ideal Fishing Day
attacks.text: Put an Item card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Slow-witted and oblivious, this Pokémon won't feel any pain if its tail gets eaten. It won't notice when its tail grows back, either.

name: Slowbro

attacks.name: Tumbling Tackle
attacks.text: Both Active Pokémon are now Asleep.

attacks.name: Twilight Inspiration
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining. Take 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Slowpoke became Slowbro when a Shellder bit on to its tail. Sweet flavors seeping from the tail make the Shellder feel as if its life is a dream.

name: Magikarp

attacks.name: Lively Grouping
attacks.text: Search your deck for any number of Magikarp, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Raging Fin
