attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Magikarp and Gyarados in your discard pile.

flavorText: It is virtually worthless in terms of both power and speed. It is the most weak and pathetic Pokémon in the world.

name: Gyarados

attacks.name: Wreak Havoc
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Wild Splash
attacks.text: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck.

flavorText: It has an extremely aggressive nature. The Hyper Beam it shoots from its mouth totally incinerates all targets.

name: Lapras

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

name: Articuno

abilities.name: Ice Symbol
abilities.text: Your Basic Water Pokémon's attacks, except any Articuno, do 10 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It's said that this Pokémon's beautiful blue wings are made of ice. Articuno flies over snowy mountains, its long tail fluttering along behind it.

name: Wimpod

abilities.name: Punk Out
abilities.text: If your opponent has any Pokémon V in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

flavorText: It's nature's cleaner—it eats anything and everything, including garbage and rotten things. The ground near its nest is always clean.

name: Golisopod

attacks.name: First Impression
attacks.text: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: It will do anything to win, taking advantage of every opening and finishing opponents off with the small claws on its front legs.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Buddy Bolt
attacks.text: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Wild Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.

name: Zapdos

abilities.name: Lightning Symbol
abilities.text: Your Basic Lightning Pokémon's attacks, except any Zapdos, do 10 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: This Pokémon has complete control over electricity. There are tales of Zapdos nesting in the dark depths of pitch-black thunderclouds.

name: Mewtwo V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Transfer Break
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Mewtwo VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psy Purge
attacks.text: Discard up to 3 Psychic Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 90 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Star Raid
attacks.text: This attack does 120 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon V. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Natu

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is extremely good at climbing tree trunks and likes to eat the new sprouts on the trees.

name: Xatu

attacks.name: Pinpoint Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 90 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon V. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: They say that it stays still and quiet because it is seeing both the past and future at the same time.

name: Lunatone

attacks.name: Cycle Draw
attacks.text: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Moon Kinesis
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The phase of the moon apparently has some effect on its power. It's active on the night of a full moon.

name: Sylveon

attacks.name: Souvenir
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Wonder Flash
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Dragon Pokémon, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: By releasing enmity-erasing waves from its ribbonlike feelers, Sylveon stops any conflict.

name: Onix

attacks.name: Rock Tomb
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Raging Swing
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: As it digs through the ground, it absorbs many hard objects. This is what makes its body so solid.

name: Larvitar

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Born deep underground, it comes aboveground and becomes a pupa once it has finished eating the surrounding soil.

name: Pupitar

attacks.name: Crashing Bullet
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Even sealed in its shell, it can move freely. Hard and fast, it has outstanding destructive power.

name: Solrock

abilities.name: Sun Energy
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Psychic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Lunatone.

flavorText: When it rotates itself, it gives off light similar to the sun, thus blinding its foes.

name: Conkeldurr V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Counter
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does that much more damage.

attacks.name: Dynamic Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 90 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Alolan Rattata

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Night after night, they sneak into people's homes seeking food. A massive outbreak of them has become an issue of public concern.

name: Alolan Raticate

attacks.name: Chase Up
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Super Fang
attacks.text: Put damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 10.

flavorText: It commands a nest of Rattata. Different nests don't get along, whipping up severe fights over feeding grounds.

name: Tyranitar

attacks.name: Raging Crash
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its body can't be harmed by any sort of attack, so it is very eager to make challenges against enemies.

name: Steelix

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Iron Buster
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It is said that if an Onix lives for over 100 years, its composition changes to become diamond-like.

name: Meltan

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It melts particles of iron and other metals found in the subsoil, so it can absorb them into its body of molten steel.

name: Melmetal

attacks.name: Swinging Smash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Revered long ago for its capacity to create iron from nothing, for some reason it has come back to life after 3,000 years.

name: Melmetal V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Arm Charge
attacks.text: You may attach a Metal Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.

name: Melmetal VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Juggernaut
attacks.text: This attack does 60 more damage for each extra Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon (in addition to this attack's cost). You can't add more than 120 damage in this way.

name: Dragonite V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Dragonite VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

attacks.name: Draconic Star
attacks.text: Look at the top 12 cards of your deck and attach any number of Water or Lightning Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Chansey

attacks.name: Delicious Egg
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The egg Chansey carries is not only delicious but also packed with nutrition. It's used as a high-class cooking ingredient.

name: Blissey

attacks.name: Enriching Egg
attacks.text: Heal all damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Whenever a Blissey finds a weakened Pokémon, it will share its egg and offer its care until the other Pokémon is all better.

name: Ditto

abilities.name: Sudden Transformation
abilities.text: This Pokémon can use the attacks of any Basic Pokémon in your discard pile, except for Pokémon with a Rule Box (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes). (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

flavorText: It can reconstitute its entire cellular structure to change into what it sees, but it returns to normal when it relaxes.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Whiny Voice
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

flavorText: It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.

name: Snorlax

abilities.name: Block
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent's Active Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Collapse
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

name: Aipom

attacks.name: Bustle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It searches for prey from the tops of trees. When it spots its favorite food, Bounsweet, Aipom gets excited and pounces.

name: Ambipom

abilities.name: Primate Dexterity
abilities.text: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

attacks.name: Full Tilt Fling
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It uses its tails for everything. If it wraps both of its tails around you and gives you a squeeze, that's proof it really likes you.

name: Slaking V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Kinda Lazy
abilities.text: If you have exactly 2, 4, or 6 Prize cards remaining, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Bidoof

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It constantly gnaws on logs and rocks to whittle down its front teeth. It nests alongside water.

name: Bibarel

abilities.name: Reassuring Dam
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, cards in your deck can't be discarded by effects of your opponent's attacks, Abilities, Item cards, or Supporter cards.

flavorText: It makes its nest by damming streams with bark and mud. It is known as an industrious worker.

name: Pidove

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Where people go, these Pokémon follow. If you're scattering food for them, be careful— several hundred of them can gather at once.

name: Tranquill

attacks.name: Aerial Ace
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It can fly moderately quickly. No matter how far it travels, it can always find its way back to its master and its nest.

name: Unfezant

attacks.name: Hurricane Wing
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Unfezant are exceptional fliers. The females are known for their stamina, while the males outclass them in terms of speed.

name: Blanche
rules: Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, flip a coin. If heads, attach a Water Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Candela
rules: Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, flip a coin. If heads, attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Egg Incubator
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, put it onto your Bench, and shuffle your deck. If tails, put this Egg Incubator on the bottom of your deck instead of in the discard pile.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Lure Module
rules: Each player reveals the top 3 cards of their deck and puts all Pokémon they find there into their hand. Then, each player shuffles the other cards back into their deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: PokéStop
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may discard 3 cards from the top of their deck. If a player discarded any Item cards in this way, they put those Item cards into their hand.

name: Rare Candy
rules: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Spark
rules: Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, flip a coin. If heads, attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Alolan Exeggutor V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Growing Tall
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 5 Grass Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Head Swing
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Mewtwo V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Transfer Break
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Conkeldurr V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Counter
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does that much more damage.

attacks.name: Dynamic Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 90 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Conkeldurr V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Counter
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does that much more damage.

attacks.name: Dynamic Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 90 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Melmetal V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Arm Charge
attacks.text: You may attach a Metal Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.

name: Dragonite V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Slaking V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Kinda Lazy
abilities.text: If you have exactly 2, 4, or 6 Prize cards remaining, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Professor's Research
rules: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.
flavorText: Professor Willow

name: Mewtwo VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psy Purge
attacks.text: Discard up to 3 Psychic Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 90 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Star Raid
attacks.text: This attack does 120 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon V. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Melmetal VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Juggernaut
attacks.text: This attack does 60 more damage for each extra Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon (in addition to this attack's cost). You can't add more than 120 damage in this way.

name: Dragonite VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

attacks.name: Draconic Star
attacks.text: Look at the top 12 cards of your deck and attach any number of Water or Lightning Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Blanche
rules: Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, flip a coin. If heads, attach a Water Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Candela
rules: Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, flip a coin. If heads, attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Professor's Research
rules: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.
flavorText: Professor Willow

name: Spark
rules: Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, flip a coin. If heads, attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Mewtwo VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psy Purge
attacks.text: Discard up to 3 Psychic Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 90 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Star Raid
attacks.text: This attack does 120 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon V. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Egg Incubator
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, put it onto your Bench, and shuffle your deck. If tails, put this Egg Incubator on the bottom of your deck instead of in the discard pile.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Lure Module
rules: Each player reveals the top 3 cards of their deck and puts all Pokémon they find there into their hand. Then, each player shuffles the other cards back into their deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Special Delivery Bidoof

attacks.name: Happy Delivery
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It constantly gnaws on logs and rocks to whittle down its front teeth. It nests alongside water.

name: Hisuian Basculegion

attacks.name: Grudge Dive
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon during their last turn, this attack does 90 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Clads itself in the souls of comrades that perished before fulfilling their goals of journeying upstream. No other species throughout all Hisui's rivers is Basculegion's equal.

name: Wyrdeer

abilities.name: Hurried Gait
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw a card.

attacks.name: Extrasensory
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: The black orbs shine with an uncanny light when the Pokémon is erecting invisible barriers. The fur shed from its beard retains heat well and is a highly useful material for winter clothing.

name: Hisuian Samurott

abilities.name: Wily Stance
abilities.text: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Dark Mastery
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Hard of heart and deft of blade, this rare form of Samurott is a product of the Pokémon's evolution in the region of Hisui. Its turbulent blows crash into foes like ceaseless pounding waves.

name: Magnezone

abilities.name: Giga Magnet
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck and attach any number of Metal Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: Some say that Magnezone receives signals from space via the antenna on its head and that it's being controlled by some mysterious being.

name: Toxel

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It manipulates the chemical makeup of its poison to produce electricity. The voltage is weak, but it can cause a tingling paralysis.

name: Oricorio

attacks.name: Mixed Call
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon and a Supporter card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This Oricorio has sipped purple nectar. Some dancers use its graceful, elegant dancing as inspiration.

name: Sylveon

attacks.name: Time Out Kick
attacks.text: You may put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

attacks.name: Symphony Whip
attacks.text: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: There's a Galarian fairy tale that describes a beautiful Sylveon vanquishing a dreadful dragon Pokémon.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Be Prepared
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

name: Kleavor V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Axe Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Kleavor VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Axe Break
attacks.text: This attack also does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon V. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Rampaging Star
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Professor's Research
rules: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Mewtwo V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Transfer Break
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Melmetal V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Arm Charge
attacks.text: You may attach a Metal Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.

name: Alolan Exeggutor V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Growing Tall
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 5 Grass Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Head Swing
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Spark
rules: Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, flip a coin. If heads, attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Blanche
rules: Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, flip a coin. If heads, attach a Water Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Candela
rules: Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, flip a coin. If heads, attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Mewtwo V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Transfer Break
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Radiant Eevee
rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.

attacks.name: Twinkle Gathering
attacks.text: Search your deck for a number of cards up to the number of different types of Pokémon you have in play and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.

name: Bulbasaur
flavorText: There is a plant seed on its back right from the day this Pokémon is born. The seed slowly grows larger.

name: Charmander

attacks.name: Tail on Fire
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It has a preference for hot things. When it rains, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

name: Squirtle
flavorText: When it retracts its long neck into its shell, it squirts out water with vigorous force.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Gift Delivery
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy

name: Hisuian Typhlosion V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Petrifying Flame
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

name: Hisuian Decidueye V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mountain Hunt
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Close-Quarters Shooting
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Hisuian Samurott V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Basket Crash
attacks.text: Discard up to 2 Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Lumineon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Luminous Sign
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Aqua Return
attacks.text: Shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

name: Oddish
flavorText: If exposed to moonlight, it starts to move. It roams far and wide at night to scatter its seeds.

name: Gloom

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor. The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.

name: Vileplume

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Allergy Storm
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Supporter cards from their hand. If tails, during your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

flavorText: It has the world's largest petals. With every step, the petals shake out heavy clouds of toxic pollen.

name: Paras

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The mushrooms, known as tochukaso, are controlling the bug. Even if the bug bugs the mushrooms, they tell it to bug off.

name: Parasect

abilities.name: Lethargy Spores
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may make both Active Pokémon Asleep and Poisoned.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: The bug is mostly dead, with the mushroom on its back having become the main body. If the mushroom comes off, the bug stops moving.

name: Wurmple

attacks.name: Creepy-Crawly Congregation
attacks.text: Search your deck for any number of Wurmple, Silcoon, Beautifly, Cascoon, and Dustox, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It lives among the tall grass and in forests. It repels attacks by raising up the spikes on its rear.

name: Silcoon

attacks.name: Entangling String
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It drinks dew that collects on its silk and waits for evolution. Its hard cocoon repels attacks.

name: Beautifly

abilities.name: Stoked Straw
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It has an aggressive nature. It stabs prey with its long, narrow mouth to drain the prey's fluids.

name: Cascoon

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks if that damage is 60 or less.

flavorText: It never forgets any attack it endured while in the cocoon. After evolution, it seeks payback.

name: Dustox

attacks.name: Nadir Powder
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 8 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.

flavorText: Tends to be drawn to bonfires on dark nights. Difficult to chase away from settlements because of the way it scatters highly toxic scales.

name: Seedot

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: If it remains still, it looks just like a real nut. It delights in surprising foraging Pokémon.

name: Nuzleaf

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: You may have your opponent switch their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It lives deep in forests. With the leaf on its head, it makes a flute whose song makes listeners uneasy.

name: Shiftry

attacks.name: Fan Tornado
attacks.text: You may have your opponent switch their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tearing Gust
attacks.text: Put this Pokémon and all attached cards in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: A Pokémon that was feared as a forest guardian. It can read the foe's mind and take preemptive action.

name: Roselia

attacks.name: Soothing Scent
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its flowers give off a relaxing fragrance. The stronger its aroma, the healthier the Roselia is.

name: Roserade

attacks.name: Poisonous Whip
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Assassin's Rose
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack also does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: After captivating opponents with its sweet scent, it lashes them with its thorny whips.

name: Phantump
flavorText: After a lost child perished in the forest, their spirit possessed a tree stump, causing the spirit's rebirth as this Pokémon.

name: Trevenant

abilities.name: Elder Tree Barrier
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon V, your opponent can't take any Prize cards for it.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: People fear it due to a belief that it devours any who try to cut down trees in its forest, but to the Pokémon it shares its woods with, it's kind.

name: Blipbug
flavorText: A constant collector of information, this Pokémon is very smart. Very strong is what it isn't.

name: Dottler

attacks.name: Barrier Attack
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It barely moves, but it's still alive. Hiding in its shell without food or water seems to have awakened its psychic powers.

name: Orbeetle

abilities.name: Jamming Attachment
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may attach up to 3 Energy cards from your opponent's discard pile to your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Mysterious Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It's famous for its high level of intelligence, and the large size of its brain is proof that it also possesses immense psychic power.

name: Slugma

attacks.name: Draw In
attacks.text: Attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is made of magma. If it doesn't keep moving, its body will cool and harden.

name: Magcargo

attacks.name: Lost Volcano
attacks.text: Put all Energy attached to this Pokémon in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: Its body is as hot as lava and is always billowing. Flames will occasionally burst from its shell.

name: Torkoal

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It burns coal inside its shell for energy. It blows out black soot if it is endangered.

name: Litwick

attacks.name: Kindling Panic
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: The flame on its head keeps its body slightly warm. This Pokémon takes lost children by the hand to guide them to the spirit world.

name: Lampent

attacks.name: Flickering Glow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon appears just before someone passes away, so it's feared as an emissary of death.

name: Chandelure

abilities.name: Mountain Roasting
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon haunts dilapidated mansions. It sways its arms to hypnotize opponents with the ominous dancing of its flames.

name: Delphox V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Eerie Glow
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

attacks.name: Magical Fire
attacks.text: Put 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon in the Lost Zone. This attack also does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Litleo

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: This hot-blooded Pokémon is filled with curiosity. When it gets angry or starts fighting, its short mane gets hot.

name: Pyroar

abilities.name: Scorching Aura
abilities.text: During Pokémon Checkup, put 4 damage counters on your opponent's Burned Pokémon instead of 2.

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: The temperature of its breath is over 10,000 degrees Fahrenheit, but Pyroar doesn't use it on its prey. This Pokémon prefers to eat raw meat.

name: Poliwag

attacks.name: Splashing Dodge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: For Poliwag, swimming is easier than walking. The swirl pattern on its belly is actually part of the Pokémon's innards showing through the skin.

name: Poliwhirl

attacks.name: Double Smash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Staring at the swirl on its belly causes drowsiness. This trait of Poliwhirl's has been used in place of lullabies to get children to go to sleep.

name: Politoed

attacks.name: Lordly Songleader
attacks.text: If Poliwag is on your Bench, this attack does 60 more damage. If Poliwhirl is on your Bench, this attack does 90 more damage. If Poliwrath is on your Bench, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: At nightfall, these Pokémon appear on the shores of lakes. They announce their territorial claims by letting out cries that sound like shouting.

name: Seel
flavorText: Thanks to its thick fat, cold seas don't bother it at all, but it gets tired pretty easily in warm waters.

name: Dewgong

attacks.name: Swim Freely
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Floe Return
attacks.text: Shuffle any amount of Water Energy from your Pokémon into your deck. This attack does 40 damage for each card you shuffled into your deck in this way.

flavorText: It sunbathes on the beach after meals. The rise in its body temperature helps its digestion.

name: Horsea

attacks.name: Reverse Thrust
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Horsea makes its home in oceans with gentle currents. If this Pokémon is under attack, it spits out pitch-black ink and escapes.

name: Seadra

attacks.name: Swim Freely
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Jet
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It's the males that raise the offspring. While Seadra are raising young, the spines on their backs secrete thicker and stronger poison.

name: Kingdra

abilities.name: Seething Currents
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may have either player shuffle their hand and put it on the bottom of their deck. If that player put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, they draw 4 cards.

flavorText: With the arrival of a storm at sea, this Pokémon will show itself on the surface. When a Kingdra and a Dragonite meet, a fierce battle ensues.

name: Luvdisc

attacks.name: Matching Look
attacks.text: Each player reveals the top 2 cards of their deck, then draws those cards.

flavorText: Luvdisc makes its home in coral reefs in warm seas. It especially likes sleeping in the space between Corsola's branches.

name: Shellos
flavorText: There's speculation that its appearance is determined by what it eats, but the truth remains elusive.

name: Finneon

abilities.name: Oceanic Accompaniment
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Water Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon that has the Swim Freely attack.

flavorText: It lures in prey with its shining tail fins. It stays near the surface during the day and moves to the depths when night falls.

name: Lumineon

attacks.name: Swim Freely
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: They traverse the deep waters as if crawling over the seafloor. The fantastic lights of its fins shine like stars in the night sky.

name: Snover
flavorText: One is likely to encounter this Pokémon while out in the snow. There are stories of Snover appearing in human settlements but doing no harm—rather, they bond with the children.

name: Abomasnow

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: If it sees any packs of Darumaka going after Snover, it chases them off, swinging its sizable arms like hammers.

name: Hisuian Basculin

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Though it differs from other Basculin in several respects, including demeanor—this one is gentle—I have categorized it as a regional form given the vast array of shared qualities.

name: Hisuian Basculegion

attacks.name: Upstream Spirits
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each basic Energy card in your discard pile. Then, shuffle those cards into your deck.

attacks.name: Water Shot
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Clads itself in the souls of comrades that perished before fulfilling their goals of journeying upstream. No other species throughout all Hisui's rivers is Basculegion's equal.

name: Ducklett
flavorText: They are better at swimming than flying, and they happily eat their favorite food, peat moss, as they dive underwater.

name: Swanna

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.

name: Kyurem V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rapid Freeze
attacks.text: Attach any number of Water Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Kyurem VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Glaciated World
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may discard the top card of your deck. If that card is a Water Energy card, attach it to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Max Frost
attacks.text: You may discard any amount of Water Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 50 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Cramorant

abilities.name: Lost Provisions
abilities.text: If you have 4 or more cards in the Lost Zone, ignore all Energy in this Pokémon's attack costs.

attacks.name: Spit Innocently
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

flavorText: It's so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it's battling midfight.

name: Glastrier

attacks.name: Freeze Down
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Glastrier emits intense cold from its hooves. It's also a belligerent Pokémon—anything it wants, it takes by force.

name: Pikachu

abilities.name: Pika Dash
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.

name: Raichu

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Ace Spark
attacks.text: If you have used your VSTAR Power, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high-voltage power.

name: Electrike

attacks.name: Thunder Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. It gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

name: Manectric

attacks.name: Assault Laser
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It stimulates its own muscles with electricity, so it can move quickly. It eases its soreness with electricity, too, so it can recover quickly as well.

name: Magnezone V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Magnetic Tension
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Splitting Beam
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Magnezone VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Magnetic Grip
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Item cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Electro Star
attacks.text: This attack does 90 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Rotom V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Instant Charge
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw 3 cards. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

attacks.name: Scrap Short
attacks.text: Put any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile in the Lost Zone. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you put in the Lost Zone in this way.

name: Tynamo

attacks.name: Call Sign
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Lightning Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: One alone can emit only a trickle of electricity, so a group of them gathers to unleash a powerful electric shock.

name: Eelektrik

abilities.name: Ad Hoc Shock
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.

name: Eelektross

attacks.name: Coil
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's attacks do 120 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Extreme Current
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

name: Clefairy

abilities.name: Moon-Watching Party
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, for each of your Benched Clefairy, you may search your deck for a Psychic Energy card and attach it to that Clefairy. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Wonder Storm
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Psychic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It can be found in quiet mountain areas on a full moon's night. Its dancing and its tiny, faintly glowing wings confer upon it a lovely fairylike quality.

name: Clefable

abilities.name: Spirit Charm
abilities.text: All of your Pokémon take 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Dragon Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). You can't apply more than 1 Spirit Charm Ability at a time.

flavorText: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

name: Gastly

attacks.name: Furtive Drop
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Born from gases, anyone would faint if engulfed by its gaseous body, which contains poison.

name: Haunter

attacks.name: Cursed Drop
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: This frightful, malevolent spirit can glide through walls, appearing wherever it likes. According to rumor, victims of a Haunter's lick will wither to death day by day.

name: Gengar

abilities.name: Netherworld Gate
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may put it onto your Bench. If you do, put 3 damage counters on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Screaming Circle
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: On the night of a full moon, if shadows move on their own and laugh, it must be Gengar's doing.

name: Mr. Mime

attacks.name: Tricky Slap
attacks.text: You and your opponent play Rock-Paper-Scissors until someone wins. If you win, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The behavior of this clown-like Pokémon reminds one of pantomime. It creates invisible walls using a force emitted from its fingertips.

name: Jynx

attacks.name: Alluring Dance
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its strange cries sound like human language. There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.

name: Radiant Gardevoir
rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.

abilities.name: Loving Veil
abilities.text: All of your Pokémon take 20 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon V (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It has the power to predict the future. Its power peaks when it is protecting its Trainer.

name: Sableye

attacks.name: Lost Mine
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you have 10 or more cards in the Lost Zone. Put 12 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: This Pokémon is feared. When its gemstone eyes begin to glow with a sinister shine, it's believed that Sableye will steal people's spirits away.

name: Mawile

attacks.name: Tempting Trap
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat. During your next turn, the Defending Pokémon takes 90 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It uses its docile-looking face to lull foes into complacency, then bites with its huge, relentless jaws.

name: Shuppet
flavorText: There's a proverb that says, "Shun the house where Shuppet gather in the growing dusk."

name: Banette

abilities.name: Puppet Offering
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand. If you do, put this Pokémon in the Lost Zone. (Discard all attached cards.)

flavorText: Resentment at being cast off made it spring into being. Some say that treating it well will satisfy it, and it will once more become a stuffed toy.

name: Cresselia

attacks.name: Moonglow Reverse
attacks.text: Move 2 damage counters from each of your Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Those who sleep holding Cresselia's feather are assured of joyful dreams. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

name: Hisuian Zorua

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: A once-departed soul, returned to life in Hisui. Derives power from resentment, which rises as energy atop its head and takes on the forms of foes. In this way, Zorua vents lingering malice.

name: Hisuian Zoroark

attacks.name: Doom Curse
attacks.text: At the end of your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon will be Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Call Back
attacks.text: Put a card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: With its disheveled white fur, it looks like an embodiment of death. Heedless of its own safety, Zoroark attacks its nemeses with a bitter energy so intense, it lacerates Zoroark's own body.

name: Inkay

attacks.name: Fickle Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It spins while making its luminescent spots flash. These spots allow it to communicate with others by using different patterns of light.

name: Malamar

attacks.name: Synchro Kinesis
attacks.text: Each player reveals their hand. If a card in your opponent's hand has the same name as a card in your hand, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Gazing at its luminescent spots will quickly induce a hypnotic state, putting the observer under Malamar's control.

name: Comfey

abilities.name: Flower Selecting
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Put the other card in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: Comfey picks flowers with its vine and decorates itself with them. For some reason, flowers won't wither once they're attached to a Comfey.

name: Mimikyu

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Worst Gift
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on all of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: It wears a rag fashioned into a Pikachu costume in an effort to look less scary. Unfortunately, the costume only makes it creepier.

name: Spectrier

attacks.name: Night Footsteps
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon and put 2 damage counters on each of them.

attacks.name: Phantom Strike
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Phantom Strike.

flavorText: It probes its surroundings with all its senses save one—it doesn't use its sense of sight. Spectrier's kicks are said to separate soul from body.

name: Enamorus V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Guardian of Love
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's Pokémon's Abilities done to each of your Pokémon that has any Psychic Energy attached, except any Enamorus V.

attacks.name: Blossom Tail
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Hisuian Growlithe

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: They patrol their territory in pairs. I believe the igneous rock components in the fur of this species are the result of volcanic activity in its habitat.

name: Hisuian Arcanine

attacks.name: Very Vulnerable
attacks.text: If you have no cards in your hand, this attack does 150 more damage.

flavorText: Snaps at its foes with fangs cloaked in blazing flame. Despite its bulk, it deftly feints every which way, leading opponents on a deceptively merry chase as it all but dances around them.

name: Poliwrath

attacks.name: Split Spiral Punch
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Splash Loop
attacks.text: Put 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: Its body is solid muscle. When swimming through cold seas, Poliwrath uses its impressive arms to smash through drift ice and plow forward.

name: Machop
flavorText: Its whole body is composed of muscles. Even though it's the size of a human child, it can hurl 100 grown-ups.

name: Machoke
flavorText: A sturdy creature boasting a robust physique and boundless stamina. Loves training above all else and voluntarily assists with tasks such as construction and clearing land.

name: Machamp

abilities.name: Crisis Muscles
abilities.text: If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this Pokémon gets +150 HP.

attacks.name: Strong-Arm Lariat
attacks.text: You may do 100 more damage. If you do, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It quickly swings its four arms to rock its opponents with ceaseless punches and chops from all angles.

name: Rhyhorn

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Strong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging tackles.

name: Rhydon

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: It begins walking on its hind legs after evolution. It can punch holes through boulders with its horn.

name: Rhyperior

attacks.name: Geo Cannon
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your deck. If that card is an Energy card, this attack does 100 more damage, and attach that card to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rocky Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: It can load up to three projectiles per arm into the holes in its hands. What launches out of those holes could be either rocks or Roggenrola.

name: Aerodactyl V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rock Crush
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Aerodactyl VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Lost Dive
attacks.text: Put the top 3 cards of your deck in the Lost Zone.

attacks.name: Ancient Star
attacks.text: Until this Pokémon leaves play, it gains an Ability that has the effect "Your opponent's Pokémon V in play, except any Aerodactyl VSTAR, have no Abilities." (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Sudowoodo

attacks.name: Joust
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Impound
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Though it pretends to be a tree, it fails to fool even children. To the touch, its body feels more like rock than tree bark. Sudowoodo's extreme aversion to water merits special note.

name: Gligar

attacks.name: Cyclone Pincers
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It flies straight at its target's face, then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.

name: Gliscor

attacks.name: Hurricane Shock
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads. If at least 2 of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its flight is soundless. It uses its lengthy tail to carry off its prey... Then its elongated fangs do the rest.

name: Makuhita

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: There's a rumor of a traditional recipe for stew that Trainers can use to raise strong Makuhita.

name: Hariyama

attacks.name: Shove
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Muscular Slap
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Hariyama that are big and fat aren't necessarily strong. There are some small ones that move nimbly and use moves skillfully.

name: Meditite

attacks.name: Focus Fist
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It eats just one berry a day. By enduring hunger, its spirit is tempered and made sharper.

name: Medicham

attacks.name: Battle Step
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Fighting Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe's next move.

name: Relicanth

attacks.name: Into the Deep
attacks.text: Put up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Rock-hard scales and oil-filled swim bladders allow this Pokémon to survive the intense water pressure of the deep sea.

name: Gastrodon

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon and heal all damage from it.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its search for food sometimes leads it onto land, where it leaves behind a sticky trail of slime as it passes through.

name: Mienfoo
flavorText: In one minute, a well-trained Mienfoo can chop with its arms more than 100 times.

name: Mienshao

attacks.name: Double Smash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

flavorText: When Mienshao comes across a truly challenging opponent, it will lighten itself by biting off the fur on its arms.

name: Landorus

attacks.name: Smashing Edge
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: From the forces of lightning and wind, it creates energy to give nutrients to the soil and make the land abundant.

name: Binacle
flavorText: After two Binacle find a suitably sized rock, they adhere themselves to it and live together. They cooperate to gather food during high tide.

name: Barbaracle

abilities.name: Lost Block
abilities.text: Your opponent puts any Prize cards they would take in the Lost Zone instead of into their hand.

flavorText: Seven Binacle come together to form one Barbaracle. The Binacle that serves as the head gives orders to those serving as the limbs.

name: Carbink

attacks.name: Lucky Find
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Item cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: When beset by attackers, Carbink wipes them all out by firing high-energy beams from the gems embedded in its body.

name: Rockruff

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: This Pokémon can bond very strongly with its Trainer, but it also has a habit of biting. Raising a Rockruff for a long time can be challenging.

name: Falinks

attacks.name: Fighting Headbutt
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness. If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: Five of them are troopers, and one is the brass. The brass's orders are absolute.

name: Stonjourner

attacks.name: Power Stone
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Fighting Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Lost Shot
attacks.text: Put the top card of your opponent's deck in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: It stands in grasslands, watching the sun's descent from zenith to horizon. This Pokémon has a talent for delivering dynamic kicks.

name: Spinarak

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Although the poison from its fangs isn't that strong, it's potent enough to weaken prey that gets caught in its web.

name: Ariados

attacks.name: String Bind
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Venomous Fang
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It spews threads from its mouth to catch its prey. When night falls, it leaves its web to go hunt aggressively.

name: Murkrow
flavorText: It searches for shiny things for its boss. Murkrow's presence is said to be unlucky, so many people detest it.

name: Honchkrow

attacks.name: Night Cyclone
attacks.text: Move all Energy from this Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: Its goons take care of most of the fighting for it. The only time it dirties its own hands is in delivering a final blow to finish off an opponent.

name: Seviper

attacks.name: Sucker Punch and Turn
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Darkness Pokémon.

flavorText: Constant polishing makes the edge of the blade on its tail extremely sharp. It's Zangoose's archrival.

name: Spiritomb

abilities.name: Cursed Message
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Chain of Spirits
attacks.text: This attack does 60 more damage for each Spiritomb in your discard pile.

flavorText: Exactly 108 spirits gathered to become this Pokémon. Apparently there are some ill-natured spirits in the mix.

name: Drapion V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Wild Style
abilities.text: This Pokémon's attacks cost Colorless less for each of your opponent's Single Strike, Rapid Strike, and Fusion Strike Pokémon in play.

attacks.name: Dynamic Tail
attacks.text: This attack also does 60 damage to 1 of your Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Drapion VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Hazard Star
abilities.text: During your turn, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Paralyzed and Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 3 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Big Bang Arm
attacks.text: This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

name: Darkrai

attacks.name: Nightmare
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Pitch-Black Blade
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: On a moonless night, a strange incident occurred in which every one of a village's inhabitants suffered nightmares. The villagers attested that Darkrai appeared before them in these nightmares.

name: Inkay

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It spins while making its luminescent spots flash. These spots allow it to communicate with others by using different patterns of light.

name: Hoopa

attacks.name: Hand of Djinn
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Darkness Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It gathers things it likes and passes them through its loop to teleport them to a secret place.

name: Radiant Hisuian Sneasler
rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.

abilities.name: Poison Peak
abilities.text: During Pokémon Checkup, put 2 more damage counters on your opponent's Poisoned Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Because of Sneasler's virulent poison and daunting physical prowess, no other species could hope to best it on the frozen highlands. Preferring solitude, this species does not form packs.

name: Radiant Steelix
rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.

attacks.name: Energy Stream
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Metal Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Destructive Finish
attacks.text: Discard cards from the top of your deck until only 1 card remains. This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It is said that if an Onix lives for over 100 years, its composition changes to become diamond-like.

name: Bronzor
flavorText: It appears in ancient ruins. The pattern on its body doesn't come from any culture in the Galar region, so it remains shrouded in mystery.

name: Bronzong

attacks.name: Gravitational Drop
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: Some believe that its bell-like cry opens holes to another world. It has been revered as a deity since ancient times.

name: Galarian Stunfisk

attacks.name: Field Trap
attacks.text: If your opponent has a Stadium in play, discard it. If you discarded a Stadium in this way, discard 2 Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Living in mud with a high iron content has given it a strong steel body.

name: Magearna

attacks.name: Windup Beam
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 60 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Built roughly 500 years ago by a scientist, the part called the Soul-Heart is the actual life-form.

name: Galarian Perrserker V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Feelin' Fine
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Treasure Rush
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each card in your hand.

name: Giratina V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Abyss Seeking
attacks.text: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put 2 of them into your hand. Put the other cards in the Lost Zone.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Giratina VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Lost Impact
attacks.text: Put 2 Energy attached to your Pokémon in the Lost Zone.

attacks.name: Star Requiem
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you have 10 or more cards in the Lost Zone. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Goomy
flavorText: Goomy hides away in the shade of trees, where it's nice and humid. If the slime coating its body dries out, the Pokémon instantly becomes lethargic.

name: Hisuian Sliggoo

attacks.name: Rigidify
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: A creature given to melancholy. I suspect its metallic shell developed as a result of the mucus on its skin reacting with the iron in Hisui's water.

name: Hisuian Goodra

abilities.name: Metal Lodging
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to each of your Basic Pokémon that has any Metal Energy attached by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon V.

flavorText: Able to freely control the hardness of its metallic shell. It loathes solitude and is extremely clingy—it will fume and run riot if those dearest to it ever leave its side.

name: Hisuian Goodra V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Slip-'n'-Trip
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rolling Shell
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Hisuian Goodra VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Moisture Star
abilities.text: During your turn, you may heal all damage from this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Rolling Iron
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 80 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Pidgeot V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Vanishing Wings
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may shuffle it and all attached cards into your deck.

attacks.name: Flight Surf
attacks.text: If you have a Stadium in play, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Lickitung
flavorText: If this Pokémon's sticky saliva gets on you and you don't clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won't go away, either.

name: Lickilicky
flavorText: Lickilicky's strange tongue can stretch to many times the length of its body. No one has figured out how Lickilicky's tongue can stretch so far.

name: Porygon

attacks.name: Branch Calculation
attacks.text: Look at the top 4 cards of either player's deck and put them back in any order.

flavorText: State-of-the-art technology was used to create Porygon. It was the first artificial Pokémon to be created via computer programming.

name: Porygon2

attacks.name: Garbage Attack
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon Tool card in the Lost Zone (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: This is a Porygon that was updated with special data. Porygon2 develops itself by learning about many different subjects all on its own.

name: Porygon-Z

attacks.name: Downgrading Beam
attacks.text: Devolve 1 of your opponent's evolved Pokémon by removing any number of Evolution cards from it. Your opponent shuffles those cards into their deck.

flavorText: Porygon-Z had a program installed to allow it to move between dimensions, but the program also caused instability in Porygon-Z's behavior.

name: Snorlax

abilities.name: Unfazed Fat
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks from your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)

attacks.name: Thumping Snore
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep. During Pokémon Checkup, flip 2 coins instead of 1. If either of them is tails, this Pokémon is still Asleep.

flavorText: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

name: Aipom

attacks.name: Mischievous Tail
attacks.text: Look at the top card of your opponent's deck. You may have your opponent shuffle their deck.

flavorText: This treetop dweller possesses a tail as dexterous as a hand. Ancient writings describe this Pokémon as a one-armed oddity.

name: Ambipom

attacks.name: All-You-Can-Grab
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. Search your deck for a number of cards up to the number of heads and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It uses its tails for everything. If it wraps both of its tails around you and gives you a squeeze, that's proof it really likes you.

name: Hisuian Zoroark V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Void Return
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Cyclone
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Hisuian Zoroark VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Phantom Star
abilities.text: During your turn, you may discard your hand and draw 7 cards. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Ticking Curse
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each of your Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

name: Bouffalant

attacks.name: Lost Headbutt
attacks.text: Put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: These Pokémon can crush a car with no more than a headbutt. Bouffalant with more hair on their heads hold higher positions within the herd.

name: Komala

abilities.name: All Just a Dream
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is Asleep and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, your opponent can't take any Prize cards for it.

attacks.name: Collapse
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It remains asleep from birth to death as a result of the sedative properties of the leaves that form its diet.

name: Skwovet
flavorText: Found throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon becomes uneasy if its cheeks are ever completely empty of berries.

name: Greedent

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Spill Out
attacks.text: Discard your hand. If you discarded 5 or more cards in this way, this attack does 150 more damage.

flavorText: It stashes berries in its tail—so many berries that they fall out constantly. But this Pokémon is a bit slow-witted, so it doesn't notice the loss.

name: Arc Phone
rules: Look at the top card of your deck. You may switch that card with 1 of your face-down Prize cards. (The cards stay face down.)
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Arezu
rules: Search your deck for up to 3 Evolution Pokémon that don't have a Rule Box, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Box of Disaster
rules: If the Pokémon V this card is attached to has full HP and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Colress's Experiment

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Damage Pump

name: Fantina
rules: You can use this card only if you have 10 or more cards in the Lost Zone. During your opponent's next turn, all of your Pokémon take 120 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon V (after applying Weakness and Resistance). name: Lost City

name: Lost Vacuum

The player who played this card discards first. If more than one effect changes the number of Benched Pokémon allowed, use the smaller number.
