name: Parasect

abilities.name: Lethargy Spores
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may make both Active Pokémon Asleep and Poisoned.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: Mushroom-lacking specimens of this Pokémon lie unmoving in the forest, lending credence to the hypothesis that the large mushroom is in control of Parasect's actions.

name: Roserade

attacks.name: Poisonous Whip
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Assassin's Rose
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack also does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: After captivating opponents with its sweet scent, it lashes them with its thorny whips.

name: Charizard

abilities.name: Battle Sense
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 3 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Discard the other cards.

attacks.name: Royal Blaze
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Leon card in your discard pile.

flavorText: It spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. It may cause forest fires by blowing flames.

name: Chandelure

abilities.name: Mountain Roasting
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon haunts dilapidated mansions. It sways its arms to hypnotize opponents with the ominous dancing of its flames.

name: Pikachu

abilities.name: Pika Dash
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Possesses cheek sacs in which it stores electricity. This clever forest-dweller roasts tough berries with an electric shock before consuming them.

name: Gengar

abilities.name: Netherworld Gate
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may put it onto your Bench. If you do, put 3 damage counters on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Screaming Circle
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Possesses potential victims' shadows in an effort to steal away the victims' lives. If your shadow begins to laugh, you must take hold of a protective charm posthaste!

name: Banette

attacks.name: Resolute Spite
attacks.text: Put up to 7 damage counters on this Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter you placed in this way.

attacks.name: Eerie Light
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Resentment at being cast off made it spring into being. Some say that treating it well will satisfy it, and it will once more become a stuffed toy.

name: Hisuian Arcanine

attacks.name: Very Vulnerable
attacks.text: If you have no cards in your hand, this attack does 150 more damage.

flavorText: Snaps at its foes with fangs cloaked in blazing flame. Despite its bulk, it deftly feints every which way, leading opponents on a deceptively merry chase as it all but dances around them.

name: Spiritomb

abilities.name: Cursed Message
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Chain of Spirits
attacks.text: This attack does 60 more damage for each Spiritomb in your discard pile.

flavorText: It lays curses by thinking wicked thoughts. Writings tell that this Pokémon was born out of the assembly of five score and eight malevolent spirits.

name: Snorlax

abilities.name: Unfazed Fat
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks from your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)

attacks.name: Thumping Snore
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep. During Pokémon Checkup, flip 2 coins instead of 1. If either of them is tails, this Pokémon is still Asleep.

flavorText: This glutton appears in villages without warning and devours the entirety of their rice granaries—such occurrences have long been counted among the gravest of disasters.

name: Castform

abilities.name: Weather Reading
abilities.text: If you have 8 or more Stadium cards in your discard pile, ignore all Energy in this Pokémon's attack costs.

attacks.name: Weather Force
attacks.text: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

flavorText: Its form changes depending on the weather. The rougher conditions get, the rougher Castform's disposition!

attacks.text: You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 150 more damage.

name: Mew VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Cross Fusion Strike
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your Benched Fusion Strike Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

attacks.name: Max Miracle
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Venonat
flavorText: Lives in the shadows of tall trees where it eats bugs. It is attracted by light at night.

name: Venomoth

attacks.name: Miracle Powder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a Special Condition. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.

flavorText: The wings are covered with dustlike scales. Every time it flaps its wings, it looses highly toxic dust.

name: Spinarak
flavorText: Although the poison from its fangs isn't that strong, it's potent enough to weaken prey that gets caught in its web.

name: Ariados

abilities.name: Hidden Threads
abilities.text: Your opponent's Pokémon VSTAR's attacks cost Colorless more. You can't apply more than 1 Hidden Threads Ability at a time.

flavorText: It spews threads from its mouth to catch its prey. When night falls, it leaves its web to go hunt aggressively.

name: Sunkern
flavorText: It lives by drinking only dewdrops from under the leaves of plants. It is said that it eats nothing else.

name: Sunflora

attacks.name: Bright Beam
attacks.text: You may discard up to 3 Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 70 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It gets energy from warm sunlight and is known for its habit of moving in pursuit of it.

name: Serperior V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Noble Light
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

name: Serperior VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Regal Blender
attacks.text: You may move any amount of Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Star Winder
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Petilil
flavorText: The leaves on its head are highly valued for medicinal purposes. Dry the leaves in the sun, boil them, and then drink the bitter decoction for remarkably effective relief from fatigue.

name: Hisuian Lilligant

attacks.name: Swelling Scent
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Grass Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: I suspect that its well-developed legs are the result of a life spent on mountains covered in deep snow. The scent it exudes from its flower crown heartens those in proximity.

name: Foongus
flavorText: No one knows what the Poké Ball–like pattern on Foongus means or why Foongus has it.

name: Amoonguss

abilities.name: Surprise Spores
abilities.text: During your opponent's turn, if this Pokémon is discarded from your hand by an effect of an attack or Ability from your opponent's Pokémon, or by an effect of your opponent's Item or Supporter cards, discard your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Hypno Hammer
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: This Pokémon puffs poisonous spores at its foes. If the spores aren't washed off quickly, they'll grow into mushrooms wherever they land.

name: Durant

attacks.name: Energy Digging
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: They lay their eggs deep inside their nests. When attacked by Heatmor, they retaliate using their massive mandibles.

name: Virizion

attacks.name: Four as One
attacks.text: If Cobalion, Terrakion, and Keldeo are on your Bench, this attack does 70 more damage.

attacks.name: Leaf Drain
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: A legend tells of this Pokémon working together with Cobalion and Terrakion to protect the Pokémon of the Unova region.

name: Chesnaught V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Needle Line
abilities.text: If your Active Chesnaught V is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if it is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Touchdown
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

name: Radiant Tsareena
rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.

abilities.name: Elegant Heal
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aroma Shot
attacks.text: This Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.

flavorText: This feared Pokémon has long, slender legs and a cruel heart. It shows no mercy as it stomps on its opponents.

name: Vulpix

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: While young, it has six gorgeous tails. When it grows, several new tails are sprouted.

name: Ninetales

attacks.name: Hypnotic Gaze
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Scorching Breath
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It is said to live 1,000 years, and each of its tails is loaded with supernatural powers.

name: Growlithe
flavorText: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

name: Arcanine

attacks.name: Flame Cloak
attacks.text: Attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fighting Tackle
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: The sight of it running over 6,200 miles in a single day and night has captivated many people.

name: Ponyta

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It can't run properly when it's newly born. As it races around with others of its kind, its legs grow stronger.

name: Rapidash

abilities.name: Heat Boost
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may discard a Fire Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. During this turn, your Fire Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.

name: Victini

attacks.name: Assisting Flame
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being's entire body will be overflowing with power.

name: Reshiram V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sparkling Wing
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: White Blaze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Fennekin

attacks.name: Lead
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: As it walks, it munches on a twig in place of a snack. It intimidates opponents by puffing hot air out of its ears.

name: Braixen

attacks.name: Flare Parade
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage for each Serena card in your discard pile.

flavorText: When the twig is plucked from its tail, friction sets the twig alight. The flame is used to send signals to its allies.

name: Delphox

attacks.name: Flare Parade
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage for each Serena card in your discard pile.

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Using psychic power, it generates a fiery vortex of 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit, incinerating foes swept into this whirl of flame.

name: Fletchinder
flavorText: Fletchinder launches embers into the den of its prey. When the prey comes leaping out, Fletchinder's sharp talons finish it off.

name: Talonflame

attacks.name: Quick Dive
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Merciless Strike
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Talonflame mainly preys upon other bird Pokémon. To intimidate opponents, it sends embers spewing from gaps between its feathers.

name: Litten

attacks.name: Reprisal
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its coat regrows twice a year. When the time comes, Litten sets its own body on fire and burns away the old fur.

name: Torracat

attacks.name: Gritty Claws
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon has full HP and would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, it is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.

flavorText: When its mane is standing on end, you can tell it's feeling good. When it isn't feeling well, its fur will lie down flat.

name: Incineroar

attacks.name: Secret Attack
attacks.text: Choose an attack from 1 of this Pokémon's previous Evolutions and use it as this attack.

attacks.name: Flare Shot
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When its fighting spirit is set alight, the flames around its waist become especially intense.

name: Alolan Vulpix V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: White Drop
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 50 more damage.

name: Alolan Vulpix VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Snow Mirage
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon. During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon that have an Ability.

attacks.name: Silvery Snow Star
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage for each of your opponent's Pokémon V in play. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Omastar V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Primal Guidance
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon that evolve from an Item card that has "Fossil" in its name and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Tentacle Lock
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

name: Articuno

attacks.name: Wild Freeze
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It's said that this Pokémon's beautiful blue wings are made of ice. Articuno flies over snowy mountains, its long tail fluttering along behind it.

name: Wailmer

attacks.name: Gulp Down
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It shows off by spraying jets of seawater from the nostrils above its eyes. It eats a solid ton of Wishiwashi every day.

name: Wailord

abilities.name: Jumbo-Sized
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Special Wave
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: It can sometimes knock out opponents with the shock created by breaching and crashing its big body onto the water.

name: Feebas

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Although unattractive and unpopular, this Pokémon's marvelous vitality has made it a subject of research.

name: Milotic

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Mellow Wave
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Milotic has provided inspiration to many artists. It has even been referred to as the most beautiful Pokémon of all.

name: Snorunt

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It's said that if they are seen at midnight, they'll cause heavy snow. They eat snow and ice to survive.

name: Glalie

attacks.name: Big Mouth
attacks.text: If you played Candice from your hand during this turn, this attack does 130 more damage.

flavorText: It has a body of ice that won't melt, even with fire. It can instantly freeze moisture in the atmosphere.

name: Froslass

attacks.name: Frosty Jail
attacks.text: If you played Candice from your hand during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: After a woman met her end on a snowy mountain, her regrets lingered on. From them, this Pokémon was born. Its favorite food is frozen souls.

name: Relicanth

attacks.name: Fossil Finding
attacks.text: Shuffle up to 4 in any combination of Unidentified Fossil and Rare Fossil cards from your discard pile into your deck.

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Rock-hard scales and oil-filled swim bladders allow this Pokémon to survive the intense water pressure of the deep sea.

name: Phione

attacks.name: Sea Feast
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Water Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It drifts in warm seas. It always returns to where it was born, no matter how far it may have drifted.

name: Keldeo

attacks.name: Four as One
attacks.text: If Cobalion, Terrakion, and Virizion are on your Bench, this attack does 170 more damage.

flavorText: When it is resolute, its body fills with power and it becomes swifter. Its jumps are then too fast to follow.

name: Dewpider
flavorText: It forms a water bubble at the rear of its body and then covers its head with it. Meeting another Dewpider means comparing water-bubble sizes.

name: Araquanid

attacks.name: Drowning Ball
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed, and discard an Energy from that Pokémon.

flavorText: It launches water bubbles with its legs, drowning prey within the bubbles. This Pokémon can then take its time to savor its meal.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Pika Strike
attacks.text: If 1 of your Dedenne used Dede-Short during your last turn, this attack does 180 more damage.

flavorText: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.

name: Raichu

attacks.name: Ambushing Spark
attacks.text: If your opponent has used their VSTAR Power during this game, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high-voltage power.

name: Chinchou
flavorText: Its antennae, which evolved from a fin, have both positive and negative charges flowing through them.

name: Lanturn

attacks.name: Strong Volt
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The light it emits is so bright that it can illuminate the sea's surface from a depth of over three miles.

name: Rotom

attacks.name: Overhaul
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.

flavorText: One boy's invention led to the development of many different machines that take advantage of Rotom's unique capabilities.

name: Emolga

attacks.name: Lucky Find
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: As Emolga flutters through the air, it crackles with electricity. This Pokémon is cute, but it can cause a lot of trouble.

name: Stunfisk

attacks.name: Mystery Bolt
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you have no Supporter cards in your discard pile. This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Thanks to bacteria that lived in the mud flats with it, this Pokémon developed the organs it uses to generate electricity.

name: Zeraora

attacks.name: Battle Claw
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It approaches its enemies at the speed of lightning, then tears them limb from limb with its sharp claws.

name: Regieleki V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Switching Bolt
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Lightning Wall
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Regieleki VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Transistor
abilities.text: Your Basic Lightning Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Max Thunder and Lightning
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Radiant Alakazam
rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.

abilities.name: Painful Spoons
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may move up to 2 damage counters from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another of their Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mind Ruler
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each card in your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It has an incredibly high level of intelligence. Some say that Alakazam remembers everything that ever happens to it, from birth till death.

name: Drowzee

attacks.name: Hypnotic Ray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It puts its prey to sleep and devours their dreams. It seems that bad dreams taste sour, so Drowzee doesn't particularly like eating them.

name: Hypno

attacks.name: Psy Call
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Stage 1 Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: There are some Hypno that assist doctors with patients who can't sleep at night in hospitals.

name: Jynx

abilities.name: Selfish Lips
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon V, your opponent can't take any Prize cards for it.

attacks.name: Psychic Assault
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: In certain parts of Galar, Jynx was once feared and worshiped as the Queen of Ice.

name: Misdreavus

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: What makes it happy is imitating the voices of weeping people and scaring everyone. It doesn't deal well with folks who aren't easily frightened.

name: Mismagius

abilities.name: Spiteful Magic
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has full HP and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Eerie Voice
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on each of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Its muttered curses can cause awful headaches or terrifying visions that torment others.

name: Unown V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shady Stamp
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Victory Symbol
attacks.text: If you use this attack when you have only 1 Prize card remaining, you win this game.

name: Unown VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Tri Power
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Star Cipher
attacks.text: Until this Pokémon leaves play, it gains an Ability that has the effect "The Weakness of each of your opponent's Pokémon in play is now Psychic. (The amount of Weakness doesn't change.)" (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Ralts

attacks.name: Memory Skip
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

flavorText: It is highly attuned to the emotions of people and Pokémon. It hides if it senses hostility.

name: Kirlia

abilities.name: Refinement
abilities.text: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.

flavorText: If its Trainer becomes happy, it overflows with energy, dancing joyously while spinning about.

name: Gardevoir

abilities.name: Refinement
abilities.text: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.

flavorText: It has the power to predict the future. Its power peaks when it is protecting its Trainer.

name: Mawile V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Pouty Slap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Chomp Down
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

name: Mawile VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Star Rondo
abilities.text: During your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon. If you do, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Sudden Eater
attacks.text: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

name: Meditite

abilities.name: Yoga Guard
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: It eats just one berry a day. By enduring hunger, its spirit is tempered and made sharper.

name: Medicham

abilities.name: Chakra Awakening
abilities.text: If you have exactly 4 cards in your hand, this Pokémon's attacks cost ColorlessColorlessColorless less.

attacks.name: Vigorous Kick
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Pokémon VMAX in play, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe's next move.

name: Chimecho

attacks.name: Dreaming Tone
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if an Energy card is attached to the Defending Pokémon from your opponent's hand, that Pokémon will be Asleep.

flavorText: Emitting ultrasonic cries, it floats on winds to travel great distances.

name: Sigilyph

attacks.name: Warning
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck. If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, you may put up to 5 Basic Pokémon onto your Bench in this way instead.

flavorText: Psychic power allows these Pokémon to fly. Some say they were the guardians of an ancient city. Others say they were the guardians' emissaries.

name: Solosis
flavorText: It communicates with others telepathically. Its body is encapsulated in liquid, but if it takes a heavy blow, the liquid will leak out.

name: Duosion

attacks.name: Cell Spear
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its psychic power can supposedly cover a range of more than half a mile—but only if its two brains can agree with each other.

name: Reuniclus

abilities.name: Persistent Cells
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, put it into your hand instead of the discard pile. (Discard all attached cards.)

attacks.name: Cell Fork
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and put 3 damage counters on each of them.

flavorText: While it could use its psychic abilities in battle, this Pokémon prefers to swing its powerful arms around to beat opponents into submission.

name: Elgyem
flavorText: If this Pokémon stands near a TV, strange scenery will appear on the screen. That scenery is said to be from its home.

name: Beheeyem

attacks.name: Psychic Arrow
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Also apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Whenever a Beheeyem visits a farm, a Dubwool mysteriously disappears.

name: Espurr
flavorText: Though Espurr's expression never changes, behind that blank stare is an intense struggle to contain its devastating psychic power.

name: Meowstic

abilities.name: Inviting Ears
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Supporter cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Females are a bit more selfish and aggressive than males. If they don't get what they want, they will torment you with their psychic abilities.

name: Swirlix
flavorText: It eats its own weight in sugar every day. If it doesn't get enough sugar, it becomes incredibly grumpy.

name: Slurpuff

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: By taking in a person's scent, it can sniff out their mental and physical condition. It's hoped that this skill will have many medical applications.

name: Dedenne

attacks.name: Dede-Short
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if 1 of your Togedemaru used Toge Dash during your last turn. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A Dedenne's whiskers pick up electrical waves other Dedenne send out. These Pokémon share locations of food or electricity with one another.

name: Indeedee

attacks.name: Smart Service
attacks.text: If you go first, you can use this attack during your first turn. Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: These intelligent Pokémon touch horns with each other to share information between them.

name: Dreepy

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: After being reborn as a ghost Pokémon, Dreepy wanders the areas it used to inhabit back when it was alive in prehistoric seas.

name: Drakloak
flavorText: It's capable of flying faster than 120 mph. It battles alongside Dreepy and dotes on them until they successfully evolve.

name: Dragapult

attacks.name: Dragon Launcher
attacks.text: Discard a number of your Benched Dreepy up to the number of your opponent's Pokémon in play. Then, for each Dreepy you discarded in this way, choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and do 100 damage to it. You can't choose the same Pokémon more than once. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: When it isn't battling, it keeps Dreepy in the holes on its horns. Once a fight starts, it launches the Dreepy like supersonic missiles.

name: Hisuian Arcanine V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Irresistible Force
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a Fighting Energy from 1 of your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rock Bullet
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Fighting Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Phanpy

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It is strong despite its compact size. It can easily pick up and carry an adult human on its back.

name: Donphan

attacks.name: Overspin
attacks.text: If this Pokémon evolved during this turn, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: The longer and bigger its tusks, the higher its rank in its herd. The tusks take long to grow.

name: Baltoy

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It moves while spinning around on its single foot. Some Baltoy have been seen spinning on their heads.

name: Claydol

abilities.name: Mystery Charge
abilities.text: You can use this Ability only if you have no Supporter cards in your discard pile. Once during your turn, you may attach a Fighting Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: This mysterious Pokémon started life as an ancient clay figurine made over 20,000 years ago.

name: Anorith

attacks.name: Fossil Drop
attacks.text: You may discard an Unidentified Fossil card from your hand. If you do, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. Anorith lived in the ocean about 100,000,000 years ago, hunting with its pair of claws.

name: Armaldo

attacks.name: Reaping Claw
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has 100 HP or less remaining, it is Knocked Out.

flavorText: After evolution, this Pokémon emerged onto land. Its lower body has become stronger, and blows from its tail are devastating.

name: Terrakion

attacks.name: Cavern Tackle
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from attacks done to this Pokémon. If 1 of your Pokémon used Cavern Tackle during your last turn, this attack can't be used.

flavorText: It has phenomenal power. It will mercilessly crush anyone or anything that bullies small Pokémon.

name: Hawlucha

attacks.name: Allure
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It drives its opponents to exhaustion with its agile maneuvers, then ends the fight with a flashy finishing move.

name: Sandygast

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Grudges of the dead have possessed a mound of sand and become a Pokémon. Sandygast is fond of the shovel on its head.

name: Palossand

attacks.name: Sandpot Trap
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Palossand is known as the Beach Nightmare. It pulls its prey down into the sand by controlling the sand itself, and then it sucks out their souls.

name: Stonjourner

attacks.name: Mystery Press
attacks.text: If you have no Supporter cards in your discard pile, this attack does 130 more damage.

flavorText: It stands in grasslands, watching the sun's descent from zenith to horizon. This Pokémon has a talent for delivering dynamic kicks.

name: Ursaluna V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Hard Coat
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Peat Shoulder
attacks.text: This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

name: Zubat
flavorText: It emits ultrasonic waves from its mouth to check its surroundings. Even in tight caves, Zubat flies around with skill.

name: Golbat
flavorText: It loves to drink other creatures' blood. It's said that if it finds others of its kind going hungry, it sometimes shares the blood it's gathered.

name: Crobat

attacks.name: Venomous Fang
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Critical Bite
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) If 1 of your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 2 more Prize cards.

flavorText: Both of its legs have turned into wings. Without a sound, Crobat flies swiftly toward its prey and sinks its fangs into the nape of its target's neck.

name: Murkrow

attacks.name: Flock
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Murkrow and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It searches for shiny things for its boss. Murkrow's presence is said to be unlucky, so many people detest it.

name: Honchkrow

attacks.name: Triple Draw
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Callous Wings
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Murkrow.

flavorText: Its goons take care of most of the fighting for it. The only time it dirties its own hands is in delivering a final blow to finish off an opponent.

name: Skuntank V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Pursuit Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If that Pokémon retreated from the Active Spot during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 120 damage instead. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Shrieking Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

name: Croagunk
flavorText: It makes frightening noises with its poison-filled cheek sacs. When opponents flinch, Croagunk hits them with a poison jab.

name: Toxicroak

attacks.name: Diving Uppercut
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It bounces toward opponents and gouges them with poisonous claws. No more than a scratch is needed to knock out its adversaries.

name: Sandile
flavorText: The desert gets cold at night, so when the sun sets, this Pokémon burrows deep into the sand and sleeps until sunrise.

name: Krokorok

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Krokorok has specialized eyes that enable it to see in the dark. This ability lets Krokorok hunt in the dead of night without getting lost.

name: Krookodile

abilities.name: Bully of the Sands
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may discard a random card from your opponent's hand. If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, you may discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon is known as the Bully of the Sands. Krookodile's mighty jaws can bite through heavy plates of iron with almost no effort at all.

name: Mareanie

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The first symptom of its sting is numbness. The next is an itching sensation so intense that it's impossible to resist the urge to claw at your skin.

name: Toxapex

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: To survive in the cold waters of Galar, this Pokémon forms a dome with its legs, enclosing its body so it can capture its own body heat.

name: Morpeko

attacks.name: Peko Blaster
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if 1 of your Dedenne used Dede-Short during your last turn. This attack does 60 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Intense hunger drives it to extremes of violence, and the electricity in its cheek sacs has converted into a Dark-type energy.

name: Beldum

attacks.name: Magnetic Lift
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card. Shuffle your deck, then put that card on top of it.

flavorText: From its rear, Beldum emits a magnetic force that rapidly pulls opponents in. They get skewered on Beldum's sharp claws.

name: Metang

attacks.name: Bullet Punch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Two Beldum have become stuck together via their own magnetic forces. With two brains, the resulting Metang has doubled psychic powers.

name: Metagross

abilities.name: Emergency Entry
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if you drew this Pokémon from your deck at the beginning of your turn and your Bench isn't full, before you put it into your hand, you may put it onto your Bench. If you do, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Meteor Mash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Meteor Mash attack does 100 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Because the magnetic powers of these Pokémon get stronger in freezing temperatures, Metagross living on snowy mountains are full of energy.

name: Radiant Jirachi
rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.

abilities.name: Entrusted Wishes
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Astral Misfortune
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out.

flavorText: It is said to have the ability to grant any wish for just one week every thousand years.

name: Ferroseed
flavorText: It defends itself by launching spikes, but its aim isn't very good at first. Only after a lot of practice will it improve.

name: Ferrothorn
flavorText: This Pokémon scrapes its spikes across rocks, and then uses the tips of its feelers to absorb the nutrients it finds within the stone.

name: Klink

attacks.name: Call Sign
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: The two minigears that compose this Pokémon are closer than twins. They mesh well only with each other.

name: Klang

attacks.name: Gear Grind
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

flavorText: When Klang goes all out, the minigear links up perfectly with the outer part of the big gear, and this Pokémon's rotation speed increases sharply.

name: Klinklang

abilities.name: Triple Gears
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: From its spikes, it launches powerful blasts of electricity. Its red core contains an enormous amount of energy.

name: Cobalion

abilities.name: Justified Law
abilities.text: Your Basic Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Darkness Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Follow-Up
attacks.text: Choose up to 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon appears in a legend alongside Terrakion and Virizion, fighting against humans in defense of the Unova region's Pokémon.

name: Togedemaru

attacks.name: Toge Dash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is Knocked Out, your opponent can't take any Prize cards for it.

flavorText: With the long hairs on its back, this Pokémon takes in electricity from other electric Pokémon. It stores what it absorbs in an electric sac.

name: Magearna V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Gear Throw
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Special Laser
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 120 more damage.

name: Dratini

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Dratini dwells near bodies of rapidly flowing water, such as the plunge pools of waterfalls. As it grows, Dratini will shed its skin many times.

name: Dragonair

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: This Pokémon lives in pristine oceans and lakes. It can control the weather, and it uses this power to fly into the sky, riding on the wind.

name: Dragonite

attacks.name: Energy Hurricane
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It's a kindhearted Pokémon. If it spots a drowning person or Pokémon, Dragonite simply must help them.

name: Noibat
flavorText: After nightfall, they emerge from the caves they nest in during the day. Using their ultrasonic waves, they go on the hunt for ripened fruit.

name: Noivern

attacks.name: Radiant Hunt
attacks.text: Knock Out 1 of your opponent's Radiant Pokémon.

attacks.name: Seventh Echo
attacks.text: Draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.

flavorText: Aggressive and cruel, this Pokémon will ruthlessly torment enemies that are helpless in the dark.

name: Zygarde

attacks.name: Shout of Power
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Born when around 10% of Zygarde's cells have been gathered from all over, this form is skilled in close-range combat.

name: Regidrago V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Celestial Roar
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dragon Laser
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Regidrago VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Legacy Star
abilities.text: During your turn, you may discard the top 7 cards of your deck. Then, put up to 2 cards from your discard pile into your hand. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Apex Dragon
attacks.text: Choose an attack from a Dragon Pokémon in your discard pile and use it as this attack.

name: Smeargle

attacks.name: Colorful Palette
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck. You may attach any number of basic Energy cards you find there to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: It draws symbols with the fluid that oozes from the tip of its tail. Depending on the symbol, Smeargle fanatics will pay big money for them.

name: Lugia V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Read the Wind
attacks.text: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Aero Dive
attacks.text: You may discard a Stadium in play.

name: Lugia VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Summoning Star
abilities.text: During your turn, you may put up to 2 Colorless Pokémon that don't have a Rule Box from your discard pile onto your Bench. (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes.) (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Tempest Dive
attacks.text: You may discard a Stadium in play.

name: Ho-Oh V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Reviving Flame
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may put it onto your Bench. If you do, attach up to 4 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

attacks.name: Rainbow Burn
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each type of basic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Spinda

attacks.name: Dizzying Spin
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Each Spinda's spot pattern is different. With its stumbling movements, it evades opponents' attacks brilliantly!

name: Swablu

attacks.name: Continuous Spin
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its cottony wings are full of air, making them light and fluffy to the touch. Swablu takes diligent care of its wings.

name: Altaria

attacks.name: Magical Echo
attacks.text: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: As it flies in a calm and relaxed manner, Altaria performs a humming song that would enrapture any audience.

name: Buneary

attacks.name: Try Bouncing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: If both of Buneary's ears are rolled up, something is wrong with its body or mind. It's a sure sign the Pokémon is in need of care.

name: Lopunny

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Lopunny is constantly monitoring its surroundings. If danger approaches, this Pokémon responds with superdestructive kicks.

name: Archen
flavorText: This Pokémon was successfully restored from a fossil. As research suggested, Archen is unable to fly. But it's very good at jumping.

name: Archeops

abilities.name: Primal Turbo
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Special Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It needs a running start to take off. If Archeops wants to fly, it first needs to run nearly 25 mph, building speed over a course of about 2.5 miles.

name: Rufflet

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its chick-like looks belie its hotheadedness. It challenges its parents at every opportunity, desperate to prove its strength.

name: Hisuian Braviary

attacks.name: Eerie Cry
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on each of your opponent's Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Screaming a bloodcurdling battle cry, this huge and ferocious bird Pokémon goes out on the hunt. It blasts lakes with shock waves, then scoops up any prey that float to the water's surface.

name: Fletchling
flavorText: Its melodious cries are actually warnings. Fletchling will mercilessly peck at anything that enters its territory.

name: Brandon
rules: You can use this card only when it is the last card in your hand. Draw a card for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Candice
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal any number of Water Pokémon and Water Energy cards you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Capturing Aroma
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for an Evolution Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. If tails, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Earthen Seal Stone
rules: The Pokémon V this card is attached to can use the VSTAR Power on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.)
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

attacks.name: Star Gravity
attacks.text: Put damage counters on each of your opponent's Pokémon V until its remaining HP is 100. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Emergency Jelly
rules: At the end of each turn, if the Pokémon this card is attached to has 30 HP or less remaining and has any damage counters on it, heal 120 damage from it. If you healed any damage in this way, discard this card.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Forest Seal Stone
rules: The Pokémon V this card is attached to can use the VSTAR Power on this card.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

abilities.name: Star Alchemy
abilities.text: During your turn, you may search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Furisode Girl
rules: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck. You may switch that Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Gym Trainer
rules: Draw 2 cards. If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn, draw 2 more cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Lance
rules: Search your deck for up to 3 Dragon Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Leafy Camo Poncho
rules: Whenever your opponent plays a Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to the Pokémon VSTAR or Pokémon VMAX this card is attached to.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Primordial Altar
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may look at the top card of their deck. They may discard that card.

name: Professor Laventon
rules: Put up to 3 Pokémon that have "Hisuian" in their names from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Quad Stone
rules:
You may use 4 Quad Stone cards at once.
• If you used 1 card, heal 10 damage from your Active Pokémon.
• If you used 4 cards, heal all damage from each of your Pokémon. (This effect works one time for 4 cards.)
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Serena
rules:
Choose 1:
• Discard up to 3 cards from your hand. (You must discard at least 1 card.) If you do, draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.
• Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon V with their Active Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Unidentified Fossil
rules: Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic Colorless Pokémon. At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play. This card can't retreat.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Wallace
rules: Draw 3 cards. Your opponent may draw a card. If they do, draw 1 more card.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Worker
rules: Draw 3 cards. Discard a Stadium in play.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Regenerative Energy
rules: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Colorless Energy. Whenever you play a Pokémon from your hand to evolve the Pokémon V this card is attached to, heal 100 damage from that Pokémon.

name: V Guard Energy
rules: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Colorless Energy. The Pokémon this card is attached to takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon V (after applying Weakness and Resistance). This effect can't be applied more than once at a time to the same Pokémon.

name: Serperior V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Noble Light
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

name: Chesnaught V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Needle Line
abilities.text: If your Active Chesnaught V is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if it is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Touchdown
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

name: Reshiram V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sparkling Wing
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: White Blaze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Alolan Vulpix V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: White Drop
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 50 more damage.

name: Omastar V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Primal Guidance
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon that evolve from an Item card that has "Fossil" in its name and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Tentacle Lock
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

name: Regieleki V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Switching Bolt
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Lightning Wall
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Unown V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shady Stamp
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Victory Symbol
attacks.text: If you use this attack when you have only 1 Prize card remaining, you win this game.

name: Unown V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shady Stamp
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Victory Symbol
attacks.text: If you use this attack when you have only 1 Prize card remaining, you win this game.

name: Mawile V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Pouty Slap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Chomp Down
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

name: Hisuian Arcanine V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Irresistible Force
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a Fighting Energy from 1 of your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rock Bullet
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Fighting Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Skuntank V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Pursuit Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If that Pokémon retreated from the Active Spot during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 120 damage instead. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Shrieking Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Pursuit Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If that Pokémon retreated from the Active Spot during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 120 damage instead. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Shrieking Poison

attacks.name: Gear Throw
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Special Laser
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 120 more damage.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Celestial Roar
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dragon Laser
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Regidrago V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Celestial Roar
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dragon Laser
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Lugia V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Read the Wind
attacks.text: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Aero Dive
attacks.text: You may discard a Stadium in play.

name: Lugia V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Read the Wind
attacks.text: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Aero Dive
attacks.text: You may discard a Stadium in play.

name: Ho-Oh V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Reviving Flame
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may put it onto your Bench. If you do, attach up to 4 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

attacks.name: Rainbow Burn
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each type of basic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Brandon
rules: You can use this card only when it is the last card in your hand. Draw a card for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Candice
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal any number of Water Pokémon and Water Energy cards you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Gym Trainer

name: Lance

name: V Guard Energy
rules: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Colorless Energy. The Pokémon this card is attached to takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon V (after applying Weakness and Resistance). This effect can't be applied more than once at a time to the same Pokémon.

name: Braixen

attacks.name: Flare Parade
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage for each Serena card in your discard pile.

flavorText: When the twig is plucked from its tail, friction sets the twig alight. The flame is used to send signals to its allies.

name: Milotic

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Mellow Wave
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Milotic has provided inspiration to many artists. It has even been referred to as the most beautiful Pokémon of all.

name: Flaaffy

abilities.name: Dynamotor
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It stores electricity in its fluffy fleece. If it stores up too much, it will start to go bald in those patches.

name: Jynx

abilities.name: Selfish Lips
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon V, your opponent can't take any Prize cards for it.

attacks.name: Psychic Assault
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: In certain parts of Galar, Jynx was once feared and worshiped as the Queen of Ice.

name: Gardevoir

abilities.name: Refinement
abilities.text: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.

flavorText: It has the power to predict the future. Its power peaks when it is protecting its Trainer.

name: Malamar

attacks.name: Rapid Strike Tentacles
attacks.text: Reveal any number of Rapid Strike cards from your hand. This attack does 40 damage for each card you revealed in this way. Then, shuffle those cards into your deck.

flavorText: It's said that Malamar's hypnotic powers played a role in certain history-changing events.

name: Rockruff

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: When it rubs the rocks on its neck against you, that's proof of its love for you. However, the rocks are sharp, so the gesture is quite painful!

name: Passimian

abilities.name: Throwing Coach
abilities.text: Your Rapid Strike Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Benched Pokémon V and Benched Pokémon-GX (before applying Weakness and Resistance). You can't apply more than 1 Throwing Coach Ability at a time.

attacks.name: Fling
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Passimian live in groups of about 20, with each member performing an assigned role. Through cooperation, the group survives.

name: Druddigon

attacks.name: Revenge
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon during their last turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: Druddigon are vicious and cunning. They take up residence in nests dug out by other Pokémon, treating the stolen nests as their own lairs.

name: Smeargle

attacks.name: Colorful Palette
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck. You may attach any number of basic Energy cards you find there to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: It draws symbols with the fluid that oozes from the tip of its tail. Depending on the symbol, Smeargle fanatics will pay big money for them.

name: Altaria

attacks.name: Magical Echo
attacks.text: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: As it flies in a calm and relaxed manner, Altaria performs a humming song that would enrapture any audience.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Oceanic Accompaniment
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Water Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon that has the Swim Freely attack.

flavorText: It lures in prey with its shining tail fins. It stays near the surface during the day and moves to the depths when night falls.

name: Gengar

abilities.name: Netherworld Gate
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may put it onto your Bench. If you do, put 3 damage counters on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Screaming Circle
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: On the night of a full moon, if shadows move on their own and laugh, it must be Gengar's doing.

name: Comfey

abilities.name: Flower Selecting
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Put the other card in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: Comfey picks flowers with its vine and decorates itself with them. For some reason, flowers won't wither once they're attached to a Comfey.

name: Machamp

abilities.name: Crisis Muscles
abilities.text: If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this Pokémon gets +150 HP.

attacks.name: Strong-Arm Lariat
attacks.text: You may do 100 more damage. If you do, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It quickly swings its four arms to rock its opponents with ceaseless punches and chops from all angles.

name: Scorbunny

attacks.name: Flaring Dash
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, draw a card.

flavorText: It has special pads on the backs of its feet, and one on its nose. Once it's raring to fight, these pads radiate tremendous heat.

name: Croagunk
flavorText: Once diluted, its poison becomes medicinal. This Pokémon came into popularity after a pharmaceutical company chose it as a mascot.

name: Weavile

attacks.name: Pursuit Claw
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon for each damage counter on that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: They attack their quarry in packs. Prey as large as Mamoswine easily fall to the teamwork of a group of Weavile.

name: Regigigas

attacks.name: Limber Up
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double Impact
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 120 damage for each heads.

flavorText: There is an enduring legend that states this Pokémon towed continents with ropes.

attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

name: Deoxys V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Deoxys VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.text: All of your Pokémon take 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon VSTAR (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

name: Deoxys VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psychic Javelin
