name: Sunflora

attacks.name: Bright Beam
attacks.text: You may discard up to 3 Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 70 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It gets energy from warm sunlight and is known for its habit of moving in pursuit of it.

name: Rapidash

abilities.name: Heat Boost
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may discard a Fire Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. During this turn, your Fire Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.

name: Kirlia

abilities.name: Refinement
abilities.text: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.

flavorText: If its Trainer becomes happy, it overflows with energy, dancing joyously while spinning about.

name: Archeops

abilities.name: Primal Turbo
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Special Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It needs a running start to take off. If Archeops wants to fly, it first needs to run nearly 25 mph, building speed over a course of about 2.5 miles.

name: Hisuian Basculin

attacks.name: Submerge Silently
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only during your first turn. During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

flavorText: Though it differs from other Basculin in several respects, including demeanor - this one is gentle - I have categorized it as a regional form given the vast array of shared qualities.

name: Cranidos

attacks.name: Stone Edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Its hard skull is its distinguishing feature. It snapped trees by headbutting them, and then it fed on their ripe berries.

name: Manaphy

attacks.name: Pulling Currents
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. Put up to 2 Basic Pokémon you find there onto your opponent's Bench.

attacks.name: Aqua Bullet
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It starts life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.

name: Togetic

abilities.name: Voice of Happiness
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may heal 30 damage from your Active Pokémon.

flavorText: They say that it will appear before kindhearted, caring people and shower them with happiness.

name: Hisuian Electrode V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Tantrum Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 100 damage for each Special Condition affecting this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Solar Shot
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Virizion V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Verdant Wind
abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon that has any Grass Energy attached to it can't be affected by any Special Conditions. (Remove any Special Conditions affecting those Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Emerald Blade
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Champions Festival
rules: Once during each player's turn, if that player has 6 Pokémon in play, he or she may heal 10 damage from each of his or her Pokémon.

name: Hisuian Zoroark V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Void Return
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Cyclone
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Hisuian Zoroark VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Phantom Star
abilities.text: During your turn, you may discard your hand and draw 7 cards. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Ticking Curse
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each of your Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

name: Ledyba

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: These very cowardly Pokémon join together and use Reflect to protect their nest.

name: Rowlet

abilities.name: Sky Circus
abilities.text: If you played Bird Keeper from your hand during this turn, ignore all Energy in this Pokémon's attack costs.

attacks.name: Wind Shard
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: At a distance, it launches its sharp feathers while flying about. If the enemy gets too close, Rowlet switches tactics and delivers vicious kicks.

name: Gossifleur

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

name: Growlithe

attacks.name: Warm Up
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Extremely loyal, it will fearlessly bark at any opponent to protect its own Trainer from harm.

name: Victini

attacks.name: Victory Dive
attacks.text: You may search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being's entire body will be overflowing with power.

name: Lapras

attacks.name: Wintry Call
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Melony cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Crossing icy seas is no issue for this cold-resistant Pokémon. Its smooth skin is a little cool to the touch.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Energize
attacks.text: Attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

flavorText: When Pikachu meet, they'll touch their tails together and exchange electricity through them as a form of greeting.

name: Chinchou
flavorText: On the dark ocean floor, its only means of communication is its constantly flashing lights.

name: Flaaffy
flavorText: It stores electricity in its fluffy fleece. If it stores up too much, it will start to go bald in those patches.

name: Tynamo

abilities.name: Levitate
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

flavorText: One alone can emit only trickle of electricity, so a group of them gathers to unleash a powerful electric shock.

name: Cutiefly
flavorText: An opponent's aura can tell Cutiefly what that opponent's next move will be. Then Cutiefly can glide around the attack and strike back.

name: Bewear

attacks.name: Split Spiral Punch
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: The moves it uses to take down its prey would make a martial artist jealous. It tucks subdued prey under its arms to carry them to its nest.

name: Pangoro

attacks.name: Knocking Hammer
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Shakedown
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Using its leaf, Pangoro can predict the moves of its opponents. It strikes with punches that can turn a dump truck into scrap with just one hit.

name: Drampa

attacks.name: Berserk
attacks.text: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Drampa is a kind and friendly Pokémon—up until it's angered. When that happens, it stirs up a gale and flattens everything around.

name: Smeargle

attacks.name: Live Painting
attacks.text: Reveal any number of basic Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 30 more damage for each type of basic Energy you revealed in this way.

flavorText: It draws symbols with the fluid that oozes from the tip of its tail. Depending on the symbol, Smeargle fanatics will pay big money for them.

name: Oddish

attacks.name: Leaf Boomerang
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: If exposed to moonlight, it starts to move. It roams far and wide at night to scatter its seeds.

name: Gloom

attacks.name: Offensive Scent
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

flavorText: Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor. The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.

name: Bellossom

attacks.name: Flower Spin
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Plentiful in the tropics. When it dances, its petals rub together and make a pleasant ringing sound.

name: Tangela
flavorText: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon's true appearance remains a mystery.

name: Tangrowth

attacks.name: Suctioning Vines
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Tangrowth has two arms that it can extend as it pleases. Recent research has shown that these arms are, in fact, bundles of vines.

name: Scyther

attacks.name: Mach Cut
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: As Scyther fights more and more battles, its scythes become sharper and sharper. With a single slice, Scyther can fell a massive tree.

name: Sunkern
flavorText: It lives by drinking only dewdrops from under the leaves of plants. It is said that it eats nothing else.

name: Yanma

attacks.name: Swoop Across
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: If it flaps its wings really fast, it can generate shock waves that will shatter windows in the area.

name: Yanmega

attacks.name: Shoot Through
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Jet Wing
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.

name: Kricketot

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: When its antennae hit each other, it sounds like the music of a xylophone.

name: Cherubi
flavorText: The deeper a Cherubi's red, the more nutrients it has stockpiled in its body. And the sweeter and tastier its small ball!

name: Carnivine

attacks.name: Festering Saliva
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: It binds itself to trees in marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.

name: Leafeon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Leaf Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Slashing Strike
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Slashing Strike.

name: Leafeon VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Ivy Star
abilities.text: During your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Leaf Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Grubbin

attacks.name: Energize
attacks.text: Attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It uses its big jaws to dig nests into the forest floor, and it loves to feed on sweet tree sap.

name: Zarude

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Triple Whip
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Within dense forests, this Pokémon lives in a pack with others of its kind. It's incredibly aggressive, and the other Pokémon of the forest fear it.

name: Calyrex

attacks.name: King's Instructions
attacks.text: You may search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Bloomshine
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Calyrex is a merciful Pokémon, capable of providing healing and blessings. It reigned over the Galar region in times of yore.

name: Charizard V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Incinerate
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Charizard VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Explosive Fire
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.

attacks.name: Star Blaze
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Radiant Charizard
rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.

abilities.name: Excited Heart
abilities.text: This Pokémon's attacks cost Colorless less for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

attacks.name: Combustion Blast
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Combustion Blast.

flavorText: It spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. It may cause forest fires by blowing flames.

name: Entei

abilities.name: Explosive Heat Dash
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Fire Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

flavorText: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

name: Simisear V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bursting Power
attacks.text: You may attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Flare Juggling
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Simisear VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fireball Fever
attacks.text: You may discard up to 5 cards from the top of your deck. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Ember Star
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy card in your discard pile. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Larvesta
flavorText: The people of ancient times believed that Larvesta fell from the sun.

name: Volcarona

attacks.name: Volcanic Heat
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Volcarona scatters burning scales. Some say it does this to start fires. Others say it's trying to rescue those that suffer in the cold.

name: Volcanion

attacks.name: Hydro Burn
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Water Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.

name: Salandit

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its venom sacs produce a fluid that this Pokémon then heats up with the flame in its tail. This process creates Salandit's poisonous gas.

name: Salazzle

attacks.name: Tail Trickery
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Only female Salazzle exist. They emit a gas laden with pheromones to captivate male Salandit.

name: Seel
flavorText: Thanks to its thick fat, cold seas don't bother it at all, but it gets tired pretty easily in warm waters.

name: Galarian Mr. Mime

attacks.name: Find It
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It can radiate chilliness from the bottoms of its feet. It'll spend the whole day tap-dancing on a frozen floor.

name: Wailmer

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It shows off by spraying jets of seawater from the nostrils above its eyes. It eats a solid ton of Wishiwashi every day.

name: Wailord

attacks.name: Bubble Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It can sometimes knock out opponents with the shock created by breaching and crashing its big body onto the water.

name: Corphish
flavorText: No matter how dirty the water in the river, it will adapt and thrive. It has a strong will to survive.

name: Snorunt
flavorText: It can only survive in cold areas. It bounces happily around, even in environments as cold as -150 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Luvdisc

attacks.name: Emotional Draw
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.

flavorText: Luvdisc makes its home in coral reefs in warm seas. It especially likes sleeping in the space between Corsola's branches.

name: Kyogre

attacks.name: Wave Summoning
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Water Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Dynamic Wave
attacks.text: Put 3 Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand. This attack does 180 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is said to have widened the seas by causing downpours. It had been asleep in a marine trench.

name: Kyogre V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dual Splash
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Aqua Typhoon
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Aqua Typhoon.

name: Glaceon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Frost Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Water Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

name: Shinx
flavorText: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

name: Shinx

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon generates electricity by contracting its muscles. Excited trembling is a sign that Shinx is generating a tremendous amount of electricity.

name: Luxio

attacks.name: Jumping Kick
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

name: Luxio

attacks.name: Shorting Spark
attacks.text: This attack does 90 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon that has a Pokémon Tool attached. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: By joining its tail with that of another Luxio, this Pokémon can receive some of the other Luxio's electricity and power up its own electric blasts.

name: Luxray

attacks.name: Electrostep
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Scar Strikes
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: Luxray's ability to see through objects comes in handy when it's scouting for danger.

name: Luxray

abilities.name: Explosiveness
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in your hand when you are setting up to play, you may put it face down as your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Seeking Fang
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Trainer cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Luxray can see through solid objects. It will instantly spot prey trying to hide behind walls, even if the walls are thick.

name: Rotom V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Instant Charge
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw 3 cards. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

attacks.name: Scrap Short
attacks.text: Put any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile in the Lost Zone. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you put in the Lost Zone in this way.

name: Rotom VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Conversion Star
abilities.text: During your turn, you may use this Ability. Discard any number of cards from your hand. Then, draw that many cards. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Scrap Pulse
attacks.text: Put any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile in the Lost Zone. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you put in the Lost Zone in this way.

name: Emolga

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: As it flies, it scatters electricity around, so bird Pokémon keep their distance. That's why Emolga can keep all its food to itself.

name: Eelektrik

abilities.name: Ad Hoc Shock
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.

name: Helioptile
flavorText: When spread, the frills on its head act like solar panels, generating the power behind this Pokémon's electric moves.

name: Heliolisk

attacks.name: Wild Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: A now-vanished desert culture treasured these Pokémon. Appropriately, when Heliolisk came to the Galar region, treasure came with them.

name: Radiant Charjabug
rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.

abilities.name: Shocking Block
abilities.text: Whenever any player attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon V, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: While its durable shell protects it from attacks, Charjabug strikes at enemies with jolts of electricity discharged from the tips of its jaws.

name: Zeraora

attacks.name: Wild Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It approaches its enemies at the speed of lightning, then tears them limb from limb with its sharp claws.

name: Zeraora V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Thunderous Bolt
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Zeraora VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Reactive Pulse
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage for each of your opponent's Pokémon in play that has an Ability.

attacks.name: Max Fist
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Zeraora VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crushing Beat
attacks.text: You may discard a Stadium in play.

attacks.name: Lightning Storm Star
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon 4 times. (You can choose the same Pokémon more than once.) For each time you chose a Pokémon, do 60 damage to it. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Pincurchin

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Continuous Tumble
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It feeds on seaweed, using its teeth to scrape it off rocks. Electric current flows from the tips of its spines.

name: Exeggcute

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Though it may look like it's just a bunch of eggs, it's a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

name: Exeggutor

attacks.name: Powerful Storm
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Each of Exeggutor's three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don't seem to be very interested in one another.

name: Mewtwo

attacks.name: Psypump
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Psychic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Limit Break
attacks.text: If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew's genes. It's said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

name: Mew V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Energy Mix
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Fusion Strike Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Psychic Leap
attacks.text: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

name: Girafarig

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its tail has a small brain of its own. Beware! If you get close, it may react to your scent by biting.

name: Lunatone

attacks.name: Cycle Draw
attacks.text: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Moon Kinesis
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The phase of the moon apparently has some effect on its power. It's active on the night of a full moon.

name: Dusclops

attacks.name: Fade to Black
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its body is entirely hollow. When it opens its mouth, it sucks everything in as if it were a black hole.

name: Tapu Lele

attacks.name: Energy Burst
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Although called a guardian deity, Tapu Lele is devoid of guilt about its cruel disposition and can be described as nature incarnate.

name: Hatterene V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Horoscope
attacks.text: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck. You may attach any number of Energy cards you find there to this Pokémon. Put the other cards back in any order.

attacks.name: Teleportation Burst
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Hatterene VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Witch's Domain
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may move up to 2 damage counters from your Pokémon to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: G-Max Smite
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Enamorus

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Loving Sympathy
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: When it flies to this land from across the sea, the bitter winter comes to an end. According to legend, this Pokémon's love gives rise to the budding of fresh life across Hisui.

name: Graveler
flavorText: It travels by rolling down cliffs. If it falls into a river, it will explode with its last gasp.

name: Solrock

abilities.name: Sun Energy
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Psychic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Lunatone.

flavorText: When it rotates itself, it gives off light similar to the sun, thus blinding its foes.

name: Baltoy
flavorText: It was discovered in ancient ruins. While moving, it constantly spins. It stands on one foot even when asleep.

name: Riolu
flavorText: It's exceedingly energetic, with enough stamina to keep running all through the night. Taking it for walks can be a challenging experience.

name: Pancham
flavorText: It chooses a Pangoro as its master and then imitates its master's actions. This is how it learns to battle and hunt for prey.

name: Rockruff

attacks.name: Invite Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon can bond very strongly with its Trainer, but it also has a habit of biting. Raising a Rockruff for a long time can be challenging.

name: Lycanroc

attacks.name: Midnight Fang
attacks.text: This attack does 80 less damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This form of Lycanroc is reckless. It charges headlong at its opponents, attacking without any care about what injuries it might receive.

name: Koffing

attacks.name: Smog
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It adores polluted air. Some claim that Koffing used to be more plentiful in the Galar region than they are now.

name: Absol

attacks.name: Lost Claw
attacks.text: Put a random card from your opponent's hand in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: Swift as the wind, Absol races through fields and mountains. Its curved, bow-like horn is acutely sensitive to the warning signs of natural disasters.

name: Purrloin

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It steals things from people just to amuse itself with their frustration. A rivalry exists between this Pokémon and Nickit.

name: Liepard

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Don't be fooled by its gorgeous fur and elegant figure. This is a moody and vicious Pokémon.

name: Krokorok

attacks.name: Dredge Up
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It buries some of its prey in the sand to use as emergency meals when its hunts are unsuccessful.

name: Pangoro

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon is quick to anger, and it has no problem using its prodigious strength to get its way. It lives for duels against Obstagoon.

name: Skrelp
flavorText: It drifts in the ocean, blending in with floating seaweed. When other Pokémon come to feast on the seaweed, Skrelp feasts on them instead.

name: Dragalge

attacks.name: Rocket Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. If this Pokémon evolved from Skrelp during this turn, put 8 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1 during Pokémon Checkup.

flavorText: Dragalge uses a poisonous liquid capable of corroding metal to send tankers that enter its territory to the bottom of the sea.

name: Hoopa

attacks.name: Assault Gate
attacks.text: If this Pokémon didn't move from the Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does nothing. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

flavorText: It gathers things it likes and passes them through its loop to teleport them to a secret place.

name: Galarian Meowth

attacks.name: Fasten Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Living with a savage, seafaring people has toughened this Pokémon's body so much that parts of it have turned to iron.

name: Galarian Perrserker

attacks.name: Sharp Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: What appears to be an iron helmet is actually hardened hair. This Pokémon lives for the thrill of battle.

name: Scizor

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Dangerous Claws
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Bulky pincers account for one third of Scizor's body weight. A single swing of one of these pincers will crush a boulder completely.

name: Aron

attacks.name: Slight Intrusion
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It eats iron ore—and sometimes railroad tracks— to build up the steel armor that protects its body.

name: Lairon

attacks.name: Wreak Havoc
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: Lairon live in mountains brimming with spring water and iron ore, so these Pokémon often came into conflict with humans in the past.

name: Aggron

attacks.name: Counter Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon equal to the damage done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Aggron has a horn sharp enough to perforate thick iron sheets. It brings down its opponents by ramming into them horn first.

name: Metang

attacks.name: Bullet Punch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Two Beldum have become stuck together via their own magnetic forces. With two brains, the resulting Metang has doubled psychic powers.

name: Pawniard

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It uses river stones to maintain the cutting edges of the blades covering its body. These sharpened blades allow it to bring down opponents.

name: Pawniard
flavorText: It uses river stones to maintain the cutting edges of the blades covering its body. These sharpened blades allow it to bring down opponents.

name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Spike Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: It's accompanied by a large retinue of Pawniard. Bisharp keeps a keen eye on its minions, ensuring none of them even think of double-crossing it.

name: Zacian

attacks.name: Battle Legion
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Now armed with a weapon it used in ancient times, this Pokémon needs only a single strike to fell even Gigantamax Pokémon.

name: Zacian V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Piercing Strike
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Behemoth Blade
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon VMAX, this attack does 160 more damage.

name: Zacian VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Break Edge
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sword Star
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Zamazenta

abilities.name: Metal Shield
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached, it takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: Its ability to deflect any attack led to it being known as the Fighting Master's Shield. It was feared and respected by all.

name: Zamazenta V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Regal Stance
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may discard your hand and draw 5 cards. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

attacks.name: Revenge Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

name: Zamazenta VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Shield Star
abilities.text: During your turn, you may use this Ability. During your opponent's next turn, all of your Pokémon take 100 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (This includes Pokémon that come into play during this turn or during your opponent's next turn.) (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Rayquaza V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

attacks.name: Spiral Burst
attacks.text: You may discard up to 2 basic Fire Energy or up to 2 basic Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Rayquaza VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Azure Pulse
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may discard your hand and draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Max Burst
attacks.text: You may discard any amount of basic Fire Energy or any amount of basic Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Rayquaza VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Azure Pulse
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may discard your hand and draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Max Burst
attacks.text: You may discard any amount of basic Fire Energy or any amount of basic Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Duraludon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Breaking Swipe
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Duraludon VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Skyscraper
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon that have Special Energy attached.

attacks.name: G-Max Pulverization
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Radiant Eternatus
rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.

abilities.name: Climactic Gate
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon VMAX and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

flavorText: The core on its chest absorbs energy emanating from the lands of the Galar region. This energy is what allows Eternatus to stay active.

name: Tauros

attacks.name: Adrena-Tackle
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has no damage counters on it, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: When Tauros begins whipping itself with its tails, it's a warning that the Pokémon is about to charge with astounding speed.

name: Ditto

abilities.name: Sudden Transformation
abilities.text: This Pokémon can use the attacks of any Basic Pokémon in your discard pile, except for Pokémon with a Rule Box (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes). (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

flavorText: It can reconstitute its entire cellular structure to change into what it sees, but it returns to normal when it relaxes.

name: Eevee V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Vee Brave
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Snorlax
flavorText: This Pokémon's stomach is so strong, even eating moldy or rotten food will not affect it.

name: Starly

attacks.name: Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. Their singing is noisy and annoying.

name: Bidoof

abilities.name: Carefree Countenance
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It constantly gnaws on logs and rocks to whittle down its front teeth. It nests alongside water.

name: Chatot

attacks.name: Cycle Draw
attacks.text: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 2 cards.

flavorText: It can learn and speak human words. If they gather, they all learn the same saying.

name: Regigigas V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Angry Whack
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon. This Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Regigigas VSTAR
rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Star Guardian
abilities.text: During your turn, if your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. They discard that Pokémon and all attached cards. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Shaymin

attacks.name: Gather Flowers
attacks.text: Shuffle up to 2 Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

flavorText: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

name: Stoutland V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Double Dip Fangs
attacks.text: If your opponent's Basic Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 1 more Prize card.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Yungoos
flavorText: Although it will eat anything, it prefers fresh living things, so it marches down streets in search of prey.

name: Gumshoos

attacks.name: Stakeout Headbutt
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon moved from the Bench to the Active Spot during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: Patient by nature, this Pokémon loses control of itself and pounces when it spots its favorite meal—Rattata!

name: Oranguru

attacks.name: Primate Acting
attacks.text: Choose a Supporter card from your opponent's discard pile and use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack.

flavorText: With waves of its fan—made from leaves and its own fur—Oranguru skillfully gives instructions to other Pokémon.

name: Greedent V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Nom-Nom-Nom Incisors
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

name: Wooloo

attacks.name: Rolling Rollout
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its curly fleece is such an effective cushion that this Pokémon could fall off a cliff and stand right back up at the bottom, unharmed.

name: Dubwool

attacks.name: Overhead Throw
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Rolling Dash
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Weave a carpet from its springy wool, and you end up with something closer to a trampoline. You'll start to bounce the moment you set foot on it.

name: Bea
rules: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck, and attach any Energy cards you discarded in this way to your Benched Fighting Pokémon in any way you like.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Bede
rules: Attach a basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Crushing Hammer
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Digging Duo
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, look at the bottom 8 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. If tails, look at the bottom 3 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Energy Retrieval
rules: Put up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Energy Search
rules: Search your deck for a basic Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Energy Switch
rules: Move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Friends in Hisui
rules: Draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Friends in Sinnoh
rules: Draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Great Ball
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Hop
rules: Draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Leon
rules: During this turn, your Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Lost Vacuum
rules: You can use this card only if you put another card from your hand in the Lost Zone. Choose a Pokémon Tool attached to any Pokémon, or any Stadium in play, and put it in the Lost Zone.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Nessa
rules: Put up to 4 in any combination of Water Pokémon and Water Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Poké Ball
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Pokémon Catcher
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Potion
rules: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Raihan
rules: You can play this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn. Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Rare Candy
rules: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Rescue Carrier
rules: Put up to 2 Pokémon, each with 90 HP or less, from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Sky Seal Stone
rules: The Pokémon V this card is attached to can use the VSTAR Power on this card.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

abilities.name: Star Order
abilities.text: During your turn, you may use this Ability. During this turn, if your opponent's Active Pokémon VSTAR or Active Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your Basic Pokémon V, take 1 more Prize card. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

name: Switch
rules: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Trekking Shoes
rules: Look at the top card of your deck. You may put that card into your hand. If you don't, discard that card and draw a card.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Ultra Ball
rules: You can use this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand. Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Elesa's Sparkle
rules: Choose up to 2 of your Fusion Strike Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a Fusion Strike Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Friends in Hisui
rules: Draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Friends in Sinnoh
rules: Draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Professor's Research
rules: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.
flavorText: Professor Rowan

name: Volo
rules: Discard 1 of your Benched Pokémon V and all attached cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Wild Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.

name: Hisuian Voltorb

attacks.name: Cheerful Charge
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only during your first turn. Choose up to 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: An enigmatic Pokémon that happens to bear a resemblance to a Poké Ball. When excited, it discharges the electric current it has stored in its belly, then lets out a great, uproarious laugh.

name: Kricketune

abilities.name: Swelling Tune
abilities.text: Your Grass Pokémon in play, except any Kricketune, get +40 HP. You can't apply more than 1 Swelling Tune Ability at a time.

flavorText: It signals its emotions with its melodies. Scientists are studying these melodic patterns.

name: Magmortar

attacks.name: Boltsplosion
attacks.text: If Electivire is on your Bench, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: When Magmortar inhales deeply, the fire burning in its belly intensifies, rising in temperature to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Oricorio

abilities.name: Lesson in Zeal
abilities.text: All of your Fusion Strike Pokémon take 20 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). You can't apply more than 1 Lesson in Zeal Ability at a time.

attacks.name: Glistening Droplets
attacks.text: Put 5 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: This Oricorio has drunk red nectar. If its Trainer gives the wrong order, this passionate Pokémon becomes fiercely angry.

name: Lapras

attacks.name: Raging Freeze
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon during their last turn, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

name: Manaphy

abilities.name: Wave Veil
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: It starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.

name: Keldeo

attacks.name: Line Force
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: When it is resolute, its body fills with power and it becomes swifter. Its jumps are then too fast to follow.

name: Electivire

attacks.name: Explosive Bolt
attacks.text: If any of your Benched Magmortar have any damage counters on them, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: High-Voltage Current
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The amount of electrical energy this Pokémon produces is proportional to the rate of its pulse. The voltage jumps while Electivire is battling.

name: Toxtricity

abilities.name: Maximum Downer
abilities.text: If all your Pokémon in play are Fusion Strike Pokémon, your opponent's Pokémon VMAX in play get -30 HP.

flavorText: It has an electrical organ on its chest. While generating electricity, it fills its surroundings with what sounds like the strumming of a bass guitar.

name: Mew

abilities.name: Mysterious Tail
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck, reveal an Item card you find there, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: Its DNA is said to contain the genetic codes of all Pokémon, so it can use all kinds of techniques.

name: Lunatone

attacks.name: Cycle Draw
attacks.text: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Moon Kinesis
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The phase of the moon apparently has some effect on its power. It's active on the night of a full moon.

name: Deoxys

attacks.name: Photon Boost
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Fusion Strike Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

name: Diancie

abilities.name: Princess's Curtain
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent plays a Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to your Benched Basic Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spike Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: It can instantly create many diamonds by compressing the carbon in the air between its hands.

name: Comfey

abilities.name: Flower Selecting
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Put the other card in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: Comfey picks flowers with its vine and decorates itself with them. For some reason, flowers won't wither once they're attached to a Comfey.

name: Solrock

abilities.name: Sun Energy
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Psychic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Lunatone.

flavorText: When it rotates itself, it gives off light similar to the sun, thus blinding its foes.

name: Absol

attacks.name: Swirling Disaster
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Claw Rend
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Swift as the wind, Absol races through fields and mountains. Its curved, bow-like horn is acutely sensitive to the warning signs of natural disasters.

name: Thievul

abilities.name: Baffling
abilities.text: If your opponent has 2 or fewer Prize cards remaining, whenever your opponent plays a Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to your Benched Pokémon V.

flavorText: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

name: Magnezone

abilities.name: Giga Magnet
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck and attach any number of Metal Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: Some say that Magnezone receives signals from space via the antenna on its head and that it's being controlled by some mysterious being.

name: Altaria

abilities.name: Tempting Tune
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, shuffle your deck, then put that card on top of it.

flavorText: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

name: Latias

abilities.name: Red Assist
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Psychic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Latios.

attacks.name: Dyna Barrier
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon VMAX.

flavorText: It can telepathically communicate with people. It changes its appearance using its down that refracts light.

name: Hisuian Goodra

abilities.name: Metal Lodging
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to each of your Basic Pokémon that has any Metal Energy attached by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon V.

flavorText: Able to freely control the hardness of its metallic shell. It loathes solitude and is extremely clingy— it will fume and run riot if those dearest to it ever leave its side.

name: Ditto

abilities.name: Sudden Transformation
abilities.text: This Pokémon can use the attacks of any Basic Pokémon in your discard pile, except for Pokémon with a Rule Box (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes). (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

flavorText: It can reconstitute its entire cellular structure to change into what it sees, but it returns to normal when it relaxes.

name: Dunsparce

abilities.name: Mysterious Nest
abilities.text: Colorless Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Weakness.

flavorText: When it sees a person, it digs a hole with its tail to make its escape. If you happen to find one, consider yourself lucky.

name: Miltank

abilities.name: Miracle Body
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon V.

attacks.name: Rout
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Miltank produces highly nutritious milk, so it's been supporting the lives of people and other Pokémon since ancient times.

name: Bibarel

abilities.name: Industrious Incisors
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Tail Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It makes its nest by damming streams with bark and mud. It is known as an industrious worker.

name: Riolu
flavorText: It's exceedingly energetic, with enough stamina to keep running all through the night. Taking it for walks can be a challenging experience.

name: Swablu

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

name: Duskull

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: If it finds bad children who won't listen to their parents, it will spirit them away—or so it's said.

name: Bidoof

abilities.name: Carefree Countenance
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It constantly gnaws on logs and rocks to whittle down its front teeth. It nests alongside water.

name: Pikachu

abilities.name: Pika Dash
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.

name: Turtwig
flavorText: It undertakes photosynthesis with its body, making oxygen. The leaf on its head wilts if it is thirsty.

name: Paras

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The mushrooms, known as tochukaso, are controlling the bug. Even if the bug bugs the mushrooms, they tell it to bug off.

name: Poochyena

attacks.name: Slight Intrusion
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It has a very tenacious nature. Its acute sense of smell lets it chase a chosen prey without ever losing track.

name: Mareep
flavorText: Rubbing its fleece generates electricity. You'll want to pet it because it's cute, but if you use your bare hand, you'll get a painful shock.

name: Leafeon VSTAR

name: Adaman
rules: You can use this card only if you discard 2 Metal Energy cards from your hand. Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Cheren's Care
rules: Put 1 of your Colorless Pokémon that has any damage counters on it and all attached cards into your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Colress's Experiment
rules: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and put 3 of them into your hand. Put the other cards in the Lost Zone.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Cynthia's Ambition
rules: Draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand. If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn, draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand instead.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Gardenia's Vigor
rules: Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, attach up to 2 Grass Energy cards from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Grant
rules: During this turn, your Fighting Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). During your turn, if this Grant is in your discard pile, you may discard 2 cards, except any Grant, from your hand. If you do, put this Grant into your hand. (This effect doesn't use up your Supporter card for the turn.)
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Irida
rules: Search your deck for a Water Pokémon and an Item card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Melony
rules: Attach a Water Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon V. If you do, draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Raihan
rules: You can play this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn. Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Roxanne
rules: You can use this card only if your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining. Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, you draw 6 cards, and your opponent draws 2 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

flavorText: The one with the best drumming techniques becomes the boss of the troop. It has a gentle disposition and values harmony among its group.

name: Cinderace

abilities.name: Crisis Power

attacks.name: Fireball Shot
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It's skilled at both offense and defense, and it gets pumped up when cheered on. But if it starts showboating, it could put itself in a tough spot.

name: Inteleon

abilities.name: Quick Shooting
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Its nictitating membranes let it pick out foes' weak points so it can precisely blast them with water that shoots from its fingertips at Mach 3.

name: Scatterbug

abilities.name: Adaptive Evolution
abilities.text: This Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.

flavorText: This Pokémon scatters poisonous powder to repel enemies. It will eat different plants depending on where it lives.

name: Pineco

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It spits out a fluid that it uses to glue tree bark to its body. The fluid hardens when it touches air.

name: Cacturne

abilities.name: Counterattack Quills
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

flavorText: Packs of them follow travelers through the desert until the travelers can no longer move.

name: Gogoat

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It can sense the feelings of others by touching them with its horns. This species has assisted people with their work since 5,000 years ago.

name: Sprigatito
flavorText: Its fluffy fur is similar in composition to plants. This Pokémon frequently washes its face to keep it from drying out.

name: Meowscarada

attacks.name: Trick Cape
attacks.text: You may put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

flavorText: This Pokémon uses the reflective fur lining its cape to camouflage the stem of its flower, creating the illusion that the flower is floating.

name: Tarountula

attacks.name: String Haul
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

flavorText: The ball of threads wrapped around its body is elastic enough to deflect the scythes of Scyther, this Pokémon's natural enemy.

name: Tarountula

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: The thread it secretes from its rear is as strong as wire. The secret behind the thread's strength is the topic of ongoing research.

name: Smoliv
flavorText: It protects itself from enemies by emitting oil from the fruit on its head. This oil is bitter and astringent enough to make someone flinch.

name: Smoliv

attacks.name: Nutrients
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It protects itself from enemies by emitting oil from the fruit on its head. This oil is bitter and astringent enough to make someone flinch.

name: Spidops ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
