attacks.name: Wire Hang
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: The thread it secretes from its rear is as strong as wire. The secret behind the thread's strength is the topic of ongoing research.

name: Arboliva

abilities.name: Enriching Oil
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: This calm Pokémon is very compassionate. It will share its delicious, nutrient-rich oil with weakened Pokémon.

name: Toedscool

attacks.name: Furious Kicks
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Though it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.

name: Heracross

attacks.name: Superpowered Throw
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: With its herculean powers, it can easily throw around an object that is 100 times its own weight.

name: Breloom
flavorText: It scatters poisonous spores and throws powerful punches while its foe is hampered by inhaled spores.

name: Tropius

attacks.name: Fresh-Picked Fruit
attacks.text: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It lives in tropical jungles. The bunch of fruit around its neck is delicious. The fruit grows twice a year.

name: Vivillon

attacks.name: Miracle Powder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a Special Condition. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.

flavorText: This Pokémon was born in a land where flowers bloom. It scatters colorful, toxic scales from its wings during battle.

name: Skiddo
flavorText: Until recently, people living in the mountains would ride on the back of these Pokémon to traverse the mountain paths.

name: Floragato
flavorText: Floragato deftly wields the vine hidden beneath its long fur, slamming the hard flower bud against its opponents.

name: Toedscruel

attacks.name: Eerie Tentacles
attacks.text: You may move an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Triple Smash
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It coils its 10 tentacles around prey and sucks out their nutrients, causing the prey pain. The folds along the rim of its head are a popular delicacy.

name: Shroomish

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It prefers damp places. By day it remains still in the forest shade. It releases toxic powder from its head.

name: Cacnea

abilities.name: Counterattack Quills
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

flavorText: It prefers harsh environments, such as deserts. It can survive for 30 days on water stored in its body.

name: Spewpa

abilities.name: Adaptive Evolution
abilities.text: This Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.

flavorText: Spewpa doesn't live in a fixed location. It roams where it pleases across the fields and mountains, building up the energy it needs to evolve.

name: Tarountula

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The thread it secretes from its rear is as strong as wire. The secret behind the thread's strength is the topic of ongoing research.

name: Dolliv

attacks.name: Apply Oil
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: Dolliv shares its tasty, fresh-scented oil with others. This species has coexisted with humans since times long gone.

name: Toedscool

attacks.name: Spore
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Though it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.

name: Gyarados ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Tyrannical Tail
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 180 more damage.

flavorText: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

name: Scovillain

attacks.name: Hot Bite
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Super Spicy Breath
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Fire Energy attached, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: The green head has turned vicious due to the spicy chemicals stimulating its brain. Once it goes on a rampage, there is no stopping it.

name: Bruxish

attacks.name: Vivid Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It grinds its teeth with great force to stimulate its brain. It fires the psychic energy created by this process from the protuberance on its head.

name: Wiglett

attacks.name: Dig a Little
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

name: Crocalor
flavorText: The valve in Crocalor's flame sac is closely connected to its vocal cords. This Pokémon utters a guttural cry as it spews flames every which way.

name: Wugtrio

attacks.name: Undersea Tunnel
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It has a vicious temperament, contrary to what its appearance may suggest. It wraps its long bodies around prey, then drags the prey into its den.

name: Skeledirge

attacks.name: Passionate Singing
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Blazing Shout
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Skeledirge's gentle singing soothes the souls of all that hear it. It burns its enemies to a crisp with flames of over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Cetoddle
flavorText: It lives in frigid regions in pods of five or so individuals. It loves the minerals found in snow and ice.

name: Charcadet
flavorText: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

name: Buizel
flavorText: It spins its two tails like a screw to propel itself through water. The tails also slice clinging seaweed.

name: Cetitan

attacks.name: Sweeping Tackle
attacks.text: This attack does 20 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: Ice energy builds up in the horn on its upper jaw, causing the horn to reach cryogenic temperatures that freeze its surroundings.

name: Clauncher
flavorText: Clauncher's claws will regrow if they fall off. The meat inside the claws is edible, but it has a distinct flavor that doesn't appeal to all tastes.

name: Clawitzer

attacks.name: Aqua Cannon
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: The cannonballs of seawater that Clawitzer launches from its claw are powerful enough to punch through tanker hulls.

name: Quaxwell
flavorText: These Pokémon constantly run through shallow waters to train their legs, then compete with each other to see which of them kicks most gracefully.

name: Quaquaval

abilities.name: Energy Carnival
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: A single kick from a Quaquaval can send a truck rolling. This Pokémon uses its powerful legs to perform striking dances from far-off lands.

name: Wiglett

attacks.name: Twisting Strike
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

name: Capsakid

attacks.name: Slightly Spicy
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Traditional Paldean dishes can be extremely spicy because they include the shed front teeth of Capsakid among their ingredients.

name: Growlithe

attacks.name: Relentless Flames
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It's very friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.

name: Capsakid

attacks.name: Increasing Spice
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Traditional Paldean dishes can be extremely spicy because they include the shed front teeth of Capsakid among their ingredients.

name: Arcanine ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Raging Claws
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bright Flame
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It's very friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.

name: Torkoal

attacks.name: Concentrated Fire
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It burns coal inside its shell for energy. It blows out black soot if it is endangered.

name: Fuecoco
flavorText: Its flame sac is small, so energy is always leaking out. This energy is released from the dent atop Fuecoco's head and flickers to and fro.

name: Charcadet

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

name: Slowbro

abilities.name: Strange Behavior
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bubble Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: If the tail-biting Shellder is thrown off in a harsh battle, this Pokémon reverts to being an ordinary Slowpoke.

name: Floatzel

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: With its flotation sac inflated, it can carry people on its back. It deflates the sac before it dives.

name: Alomomola

attacks.name: Aqua Slash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It gently holds injured and weak Pokémon in its fins. Its special membrane heals their wounds.

name: Growlithe

attacks.name: Stoke
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Fire Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It's very friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.

name: Houndour
flavorText: It uses different kinds of cries for communicating with others of its kind and for pursuing its prey.

name: Houndoom

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Upon hearing its eerie howls, other Pokémon get the shivers and head straight back to their nests.

name: Armarouge

abilities.name: Fire Off
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a Fire Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flame Cannon
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Armarouge evolved through the use of a set of armor that belonged to a distinguished warrior. This Pokémon is incredibly loyal.

name: Slowpoke

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep. Heal 30 damage from it.

flavorText: It is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.

name: Magikarp

attacks.name: Jump
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

name: Quaxly
flavorText: This Pokémon migrated to Paldea from distant lands long ago. The gel secreted by its feathers repels water and grime.

name: Cetoddle
flavorText: It lives in frigid regions in pods of five or so individuals. It loves the minerals found in snow and ice.

name: Dondozo

attacks.name: Release Rage
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Tatsugiri in your discard pile.

flavorText: This Pokémon is a glutton, but it's bad at getting food. It teams up with a Tatsugiri to catch prey.

name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Magnetic Switch
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It moves while constantly hovering. It discharges electromagnetic waves and so on from the units at its sides.

name: Magneton

attacks.name: Explosion
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 90 damage to itself.

flavorText: They're formed by several Magnemite linked together. They frequently appear when sunspots flare up.

name: Tatsugiri

attacks.name: Mise en Place
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Water Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Basic Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Curl Up
attacks.text: Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.

flavorText: This is a small dragon Pokémon. It lives inside the mouth of Dondozo to protect itself from enemies on the outside.

name: Rotom

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Research continues on this Pokémon, which could be the power source of a unique motor.

name: Mareep
flavorText: Its fleece grows continually. In the summer, the fleece is fully shed, but it grows back in a week.

name: Pawmot

abilities.name: Electrogenesis
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Basic Lightning Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Electro Paws
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Pawmot's fluffy fur acts as a battery. It can store the same amount of electricity as an electric car.

name: Pawmo

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Pawmo uses a unique fighting technique in which it uses its forepaws to strike foes and zap them with electricity from its paw pads simultaneously.

name: Miraidon

attacks.name: Lightning Laser
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Much remains unknown about this creature. It resembles Cyclizar, but it is far more ruthless and powerful.

name: Wattrel

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: When its wings catch the wind, the bones within produce electricity. This Pokémon dives into the ocean, catching prey by electrocuting them.

name: Miraidon ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Tandem Unit
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Basic Lightning Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Photon Blaster
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Much remains unknown about this creature. It resembles Cyclizar, but it is far more ruthless and powerful.

name: Wattrel
flavorText: When its wings catch the wind, the bones within produce electricity. This Pokémon dives into the ocean, catching prey by electrocuting them.

name: Hypno

attacks.name: Pendulum Influence
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose an attack from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon in play and use it as this attack.

flavorText: Always holding a pendulum that it swings at a steady rhythm, it causes drowsiness in anyone nearby.

name: Kilowattrel

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Thunder Blast
attacks.text: Discard a Lightning Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Kilowattrel inflates its throat sac to amplify its electricity. By riding the wind, this Pokémon can fly over 430 miles in a day.

name: Gardevoir ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Psychic Embrace
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic Psychic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Psychic Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon. You can't use this Ability on a Pokémon that would be Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Miracle Force
attacks.text: This Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.

flavorText: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

name: Dedenne

attacks.name: Energy Munch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It's small and its electricity-generating organ is not fully developed, so it uses its tail to absorb electricity from people's homes and charge itself.

name: Drowzee

attacks.name: Forced Sleep
attacks.text: Your opponent chooses 1 of their Benched Pokémon and switches it with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: When it twitches its nose, it can tell where someone is sleeping and what that person is dreaming about.

name: Klefki

abilities.name: Mischievous Lock
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities, except for Mischievous Lock.

attacks.name: Joust
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: In the past, noble families entrusted their vault keys to a Klefki. They passed the Klefki down through the generations, taking good care of it.

name: Ralts
flavorText: The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people's emotions.

name: Fidough
flavorText: This Pokémon is smooth and moist to the touch. Yeast in Fidough's breath induces fermentation in the Pokémon's vicinity.

name: Kirlia

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

name: Fidough

attacks.name: Springy
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: This Pokémon is smooth and moist to the touch. Yeast in Fidough's breath induces fermentation in the Pokémon's vicinity.

name: Shuppet

attacks.name: Enveloping Shadow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

flavorText: It feeds on the dark emotions of sadness and hatred, which make it grow steadily stronger.

name: Flittle
flavorText: Flittle's toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon's belly.

name: Drifloon

attacks.name: Balloon Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is whispered that any child who mistakes Drifloon for a balloon and holds on to it could wind up missing.

name: Drifblim

attacks.name: Curse Spreading
attacks.text: Put 8 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It can generate and release gas within its body. That's how it can control the altitude of its drift.

name: Flabébé
flavorText: This Flabébé rides a red flower. Immediately after birth, this Pokémon begins flying around in search of a flower it likes.

name: Magnezone ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pulse Launcher
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: They're formed by several Magnemite linked together. They frequently appear when sunspots flare up.

name: Flaaffy

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Because of its rubbery, electricity-resistant skin, it can store lots of electricity in its fur.

name: Toxel
flavorText: This selfish, attention-seeking Pokémon stores poison and electricity in two different sacs inside its body.

name: Toxtricity

attacks.name: Yank Away
attacks.text: Choose 2 random cards from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into their deck.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: The jolts of electricity it launches by violently strumming the protrusions on its chest easily exceed 15,000 volts.

name: Pawmi
flavorText: The pads of its paws are electricity-discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.

name: Florges

abilities.name: Blooming Garden
abilities.text: Your Pokémon in play have no Weakness.

attacks.name: Moonblast
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: They say that flower gardens created by Florges are constantly showered with a power that can heal both body and spirit.

name: Flittle
flavorText: Flittle's toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon's belly.

name: Pachirisu

abilities.name: Electricity Pouches
abilities.text: This Pokémon can't be Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Everyone Discharge
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Lightning Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

flavorText: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

name: Rotom

attacks.name: Junk Hunt
attacks.text: Put an Item card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Research continues on this Pokémon, which could be the power source of a unique motor.

name: Pawmi

attacks.name: Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The pads of its paws are electricity-discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.

name: Banette ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Everlasting Darkness
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Poltergeist
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 60 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

flavorText: It feeds on the dark emotions of sadness and hatred, which make it grow steadily stronger.

name: Floette

attacks.name: Magical Leaf
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage, and heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon uses red wavelengths of light to pour its own energy into flowers and draw forth their latent potential.

name: Dedenne

attacks.name: Second Bite
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It's small and its electricity-generating organ is not fully developed, so it uses its tail to absorb electricity from people's homes and charge itself.

name: Dachsbun

abilities.name: Well-Baked Body
abilities.text: This Pokémon can't be Burned. Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Fire Pokémon.

flavorText: The pleasant aroma that emanates from this Pokémon's body helps wheat grow, so Dachsbun has been treasured by farming villages.

name: Flittle

attacks.name: Dash Off
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Flittle's toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon's belly.

name: Greavard

attacks.name: Underworld Stroll
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a Supporter card you find there and put it on the bottom of their deck.

flavorText: This friendly Pokémon doesn't like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.

name: Greavard

attacks.name: Graveyard Gamboling
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each Psychic Pokémon in your discard pile.

flavorText: This friendly Pokémon doesn't like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.

name: Espathra

attacks.name: Eye Strength
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It immobilizes opponents by bathing them in psychic power from its large eyes. Despite its appearance, it has a vicious temperament.

name: Houndstone

attacks.name: Last Respects
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each Psychic Pokémon in your discard pile.

flavorText: A lovingly mourned Pokémon was reborn as Houndstone. It doesn't like anyone touching the protuberance atop its head.

name: Mankey

attacks.name: Monkey Beatdown
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It is extremely quick to anger. It could be docile one moment, then thrashing away the next instant.

name: Primeape

attacks.name: Raging Punch
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Some researchers theorize that Primeape remains angry even when inside a Poké Ball.

name: Annihilape

attacks.name: Rage Fist
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

attacks.name: Dynamite Punch
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: It imbues its fists with the power of the rage that it kept hidden in its heart. Opponents struck by these imbued fists will be shattered to their core.

name: Medicham

attacks.name: Acu-Punch-Ture
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

attacks.name: Kick Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe's next move.

name: Meditite

attacks.name: Feint
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It never skips its daily yoga training. It heightens its inner strength through meditation.

name: Riolu

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

name: Riolu
flavorText: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

name: Lucario

attacks.name: Avenging Knuckle
attacks.text: If any of your Fighting Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

attacks.name: Accelerating Stab
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Accelerating Stab.

flavorText: It's said that no foe can remain invisible to Lucario, since it can detect auras—even those of foes it could not otherwise see.

name: Sandile
flavorText: It submerges itself in sand and moves as if swimming. This wise behavior keeps its enemies from finding it and maintains its temperature.

name: Krookodile

attacks.name: Chomp Chomp Bite
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: After clamping down with its powerful jaws, it twists its body around to rip its prey in half.

name: Hawlucha

abilities.name: Flying Entry
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may choose 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and put 1 damage counter on each of them.

flavorText: Its elegant finishing moves—performed by nimbly leaping around using its wings—are polished in the forest where it was born and raised.

name: Krokorok

attacks.name: Payback
attacks.text: If your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Protected by thin membranes, their eyes can see even in the dead of night. They live in groups of a few individuals.

name: Silicobra
flavorText: Silicobra's neck pouch, which can inflate and deflate like a balloon, gets more elastic each time Silicobra sheds its skin.

name: Sandaconda

attacks.name: Sandstorm Spray
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. If you discarded any Energy in this way, your opponent shuffles their Active Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck.

flavorText: It will expand its body as much as it can and then contract itself, blasting out sand with enough force to wash away a dump truck.

name: Stonjourner

abilities.name: Exoskeleton
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The elemental composition of the rocks that form its body were found to match the bedrock of a land far away from this Pokémon's habitat.

name: Great Tusk ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bedrock Breaker
attacks.text: Discard a Stadium in play.

attacks.name: Gigantic Tusks
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: Klawf hangs upside-down from cliffs, waiting for prey. But Klawf can't remain in this position for long because its blood rushes to its head.

name: Klawf

attacks.name: Adrenaline Hammer
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Klawf hangs upside-down from cliffs, waiting for prey. But Klawf can't remain in this position for long because its blood rushes to its head.

name: Koraidon

attacks.name: Rampaging Fang
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This seems to be the Winged King mentioned in an old expedition journal. It was said to have split the land with its bare fists.

name: Koraidon ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dino Cry
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Basic Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to your Basic Fighting Pokémon in any way you like. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

attacks.name: Wild Impact
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: This seems to be the Winged King mentioned in an old expedition journal. It was said to have split the land with its bare fists.

name: Grimer

attacks.name: Super Poison Breath
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Born from sludge, these Pokémon now gather in polluted places and increase the bacteria in their bodies.

name: Muk

abilities.name: Poison Sacs
abilities.text: Your opponent's Poisoned Pokémon don't recover from that Special Condition when they evolve or devolve.

attacks.name: Toxic Strike
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It's thickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.

name: Seviper

attacks.name: Spit Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: It sharpens its swordlike tail on hard rocks. It hides in tall grass and strikes unwary prey with venomous fangs.

name: Pawniard
flavorText: Pawniard will fearlessly challenge even powerful foes. In a pinch, it will cling to opponents and pierce them with the blades all over its body.

name: Spiritomb

attacks.name: Taunt
attacks.text: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

attacks.name: Doom Decree
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out.

flavorText: It was formed by uniting 108 spirits. It has been bound to the Odd Keystone to keep it from doing any mischief.

name: Croagunk
flavorText: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

name: Toxicroak ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Nasty Plot
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Toxic Ripper
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 6 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.

flavorText: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Double-Edged Slash
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon commands a group of several Pawniard. Groups that are defeated in territorial disputes are absorbed by the winning side.

name: Kingambit

abilities.name: Leadership
abilities.text: Your Basic Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Only a Bisharp that stands above all others in its vast army can evolve into Kingambit.

name: Maschiff
flavorText: Its well-developed jaw and fangs are strong enough to crunch through boulders, and its thick fat makes for an excellent defense.

name: Maschiff

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its well-developed jaw and fangs are strong enough to crunch through boulders, and its thick fat makes for an excellent defense.

name: Forretress

attacks.name: Continuous Spin
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Rolling Shell
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its entire body is shielded by a steel-hard shell. What lurks inside this shell is a total mystery.

name: Mabosstiff

abilities.name: Intimidating Howl
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Mabosstiff loves playing with children. Though usually gentle, it takes on an intimidating look when protecting its family.

name: Bombirdier

attacks.name: Knickknack Carrying
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon Tool cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Bombirdier uses the apron on its chest to bundle up food, which it carries back to its nest. It enjoys dropping things that make loud noises.

name: Varoom

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.

name: Revavroom

abilities.name: Rumbling Engine
abilities.text: You must discard an Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders— now numbering eight—to generate energy.

name: Varoom
flavorText: It is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.

name: Iron Treads ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Triple Laser
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 3 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Cybernetic Wheels
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders— now numbering eight—to generate energy.

name: Chansey

attacks.name: Egg Rolling
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It walks carefully to prevent its egg from breaking. However, it is extremely fast at running away.

name: Blissey

abilities.name: Busybody Nurse
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Your Active Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.

attacks.name: Happy Cyclone
attacks.text: Move all Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The eggs it lays are filled with happiness. Eating even one bite will bring a smile to anyone.

name: Zangoose
flavorText: The eggs it lays are filled with happiness. Eating even one bite will bring a smile to anyone.

name: Starly
flavorText: They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. Their singing is noisy and annoying.

name: Zangoose

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. This attack does 30 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It's Seviper's archrival. To threaten those it encounters, it fans out the claws on its front paws.

name: Skwovet

abilities.name: Nest Stash
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may shuffle your hand and put it on the bottom of your deck. If you put any cards on the bottom of your deck in this way, draw a card.

flavorText: No matter how much it stuffs its belly with food, it is always anxious about getting hungry again. So, it stashes berries in its cheeks and tail.

name: Staravia
flavorText: Recognizing their own weakness, they always live in a group. When alone, a Staravia cries noisily.

name: Staraptor

attacks.name: Tailspin Away
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

attacks.name: Power Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.

name: Indeedee

attacks.name: Expert Nurturer
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: In search of happy feelings—such as joy and gratitude—Indeedee bustles around, taking diligent care of people and other Pokémon.

name: Greedent

attacks.name: Enhanced Fang
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon makes off with heaps of fallen berries by wrapping them in its tail, which is roughly twice the length of its body.

name: Lechonk

attacks.name: Repelling Odor
attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn't use it for anything other than foraging.

name: Oinkologne ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Maddening Scent
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Heavy Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Oinkologne is proud of its fine, glossy skin. It emits a concentrated scent from the tip of its tail.

name: Oinkologne

attacks.name: Leg Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Oinkologne is proud of its fine, glossy skin. It emits a concentrated scent from the tip of its tail.

name: Lechonk

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn't use it for anything other than foraging.

name: Lechonk

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn't use it for anything other than foraging.

name: Tandemaus
flavorText: The pair sticks together no matter what. They split any food they find exactly in half and then eat it together.

name: Tandemaus

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The pair sticks together no matter what. They split any food they find exactly in half and then eat it together.

name: Squawkabilly

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Green-feathered flocks hold the most sway. When they're out searching for food in the mornings and evenings, it gets very noisy.

name: Maushold

attacks.name: Family Attack
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage for each of your Maushold in play.

flavorText: The larger pair protects the little ones during battles. When facing strong opponents, the whole group will join the fight.

name: Cyclizar

attacks.name: Touring
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: It can sprint at over 70 mph while carrying a human. The rider's body heat warms Cyclizar's back and lifts the Pokémon's spirit.

name: Cyclizar

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Apparently Cyclizar has been allowing people to ride on its back since ancient times. Depictions of this have been found in 10,000-year-old murals.

name: Flamigo

attacks.name: Nosedive
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Thanks to a behavior of theirs known as "synchronizing," an entire flock of these Pokémon can attack simultaneously in perfect harmony.

name: Arven
rules: Search your deck for an Item card and a Pokémon Tool card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.
flavorText: Thanks to a behavior of theirs known as "synchronizing," an entire flock of these Pokémon can attack simultaneously in perfect harmony.

name: Beach Court
rules: The Retreat Cost of each Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) is Colorless less.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

name: Energy Switch
rules: Move a Basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Defiance Band
rules: If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, the attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Energy Retrieval
rules: Put up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Energy Search
rules: Search your deck for a Basic Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Exp. Share
rules: When your Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, you may move a Basic Energy from that Pokémon to the Pokémon this card is attached to.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Crushing Hammer
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Electric Generator
rules: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and attach up to 2 Basic Lightning Energy cards you find there to your Benched Lightning Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Judge
rules: Each player shuffles their hand into their deck and draws 4 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Mesagoza
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may flip a coin. If heads, that player searches their deck for a Pokémon, reveals it, and puts it into their hand. Then, that player shuffles their deck.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

name: Jacq
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 Evolution Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Katy
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 8 cards. Your turn ends.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Miriam
rules: Shuffle up to 5 Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck. If you shuffled any cards into your deck in this way, draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Nemona
rules: Draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Nest Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Penny
rules: Put 1 of your Basic Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Pal Pad
rules: Shuffle up to 2 Supporter cards from your discard pile into your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Picnic Basket
rules: Heal 30 damage from each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Poké Ball
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Pokémon Catcher
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Potion
rules: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Pokégear 3.0
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Supporter card you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Professor's Research (Professor Sada)
rules: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Rare Candy
rules: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Professor's Research (Professor Turo)
rules: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Rock Chestplate
rules: The Fighting Pokémon this card is attached to takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Rocky Helmet
rules: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if it is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Switch
rules: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Team Star Grunt
rules: Put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon on top of their deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Ultra Ball
rules: You can use this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand. Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Youngster
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Dolliv

attacks.name: Apply Oil
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: Dolliv shares its tasty, fresh-scented oil with others. This species has coexisted with humans since times long gone.

name: Vitality Band
rules: The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 10 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Tarountula

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The thread it secretes from its rear is as strong as wire. The secret behind the thread's strength is the topic of ongoing research.

name: Toedscool

attacks.name: Furious Kicks
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Though it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.

name: Armarouge

abilities.name: Fire Off
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a Fire Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flame Cannon
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Armarouge evolved through the use of a set of armor that belonged to a distinguished warrior. This Pokémon is incredibly loyal.

name: Scovillain

attacks.name: Hot Bite
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Super Spicy Breath
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Fire Energy attached, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: The green head has turned vicious due to the spicy chemicals stimulating its brain. Once it goes on a rampage, there is no stopping it.

name: Wiglett

attacks.name: Twisting Strike
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

name: Slowpoke

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep. Heal 30 damage from it.

flavorText: It is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.

name: Clauncher
flavorText: Clauncher's claws will regrow if they fall off. The meat inside the claws is edible, but it has a distinct flavor that doesn't appeal to all tastes.

name: Pawmot

abilities.name: Electrogenesis
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Basic Lightning Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Electro Paws
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Pawmot's fluffy fur acts as a battery. It can store the same amount of electricity as an electric car.

name: Pachirisu

abilities.name: Electricity Pouches
abilities.text: This Pokémon can't be Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Everyone Discharge
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Lightning Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

flavorText: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

name: Drowzee

attacks.name: Forced Sleep
attacks.text: Your opponent chooses 1 of their Benched Pokémon and switches it with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: When it twitches its nose, it can tell where someone is sleeping and what that person is dreaming about.

name: Dondozo

attacks.name: Release Rage
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Tatsugiri in your discard pile.

flavorText: This Pokémon is a glutton, but it's bad at getting food. It teams up with a Tatsugiri to catch prey.

name: Greavard

attacks.name: Graveyard Gamboling
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each Psychic Pokémon in your discard pile.

flavorText: This friendly Pokémon doesn't like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.

name: Riolu

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

name: Ralts
flavorText: The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people's emotions.

name: Kirlia

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

name: Fidough

attacks.name: Springy
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: This Pokémon is smooth and moist to the touch. Yeast in Fidough's breath induces fermentation in the Pokémon's vicinity.

name: Klawf

attacks.name: Adrenaline Hammer
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Klawf hangs upside-down from cliffs, waiting for prey. But Klawf can't remain in this position for long because its blood rushes to its head.

name: Mabosstiff

abilities.name: Intimidating Howl
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Mabosstiff loves playing with children. Though usually gentle, it takes on an intimidating look when protecting its family.

name: Sandile
flavorText: It submerges itself in sand and moves as if swimming. This wise behavior keeps its enemies from finding it and maintains its temperature.

name: Bombirdier

attacks.name: Knickknack Carrying
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon Tool cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Bombirdier uses the apron on its chest to bundle up food, which it carries back to its nest. It enjoys dropping things that make loud noises.

name: Kingambit

abilities.name: Leadership
abilities.text: Your Basic Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Only a Bisharp that stands above all others in its vast army can evolve into Kingambit.

name: Starly
flavorText: They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. Their singing is noisy and annoying.

name: Skwovet

abilities.name: Nest Stash
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may shuffle your hand and put it on the bottom of your deck. If you put any cards on the bottom of your deck in this way, draw a card.

flavorText: No matter how much it stuffs its belly with food, it is always anxious about getting hungry again. So, it stashes berries in its cheeks and tail.

name: Arcanine ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Raging Claws
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bright Flame
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Magnezone ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pulse Launcher
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Gyarados ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Tyrannical Tail
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 180 more damage.

name: Spidops ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Trap Territory
abilities.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost is Colorless more.

attacks.name: Wire Hang
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: No matter how much it stuffs its belly with food, it is always anxious about getting hungry again. So, it stashes berries in its cheeks and tail.

name: Gardevoir ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Psychic Embrace
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic Psychic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Psychic Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon. You can't use this Ability on a Pokémon that would be Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Miracle Force
attacks.text: This Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.

name: Miraidon ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Tandem Unit
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Basic Lightning Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Photon Blaster
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Banette ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Everlasting Darkness
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Poltergeist
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 60 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

name: Koraidon ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dino Cry
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Basic Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to your Basic Fighting Pokémon in any way you like. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

attacks.name: Wild Impact
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Great Tusk ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bedrock Breaker
attacks.text: Discard a Stadium in play.

attacks.name: Gigantic Tusks
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

name: Iron Treads ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Triple Laser
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 3 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Cybernetic Wheels
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Toxicroak ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Nasty Plot
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Toxic Ripper
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 6 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.

name: Oinkologne ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Maddening Scent
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Heavy Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.
